1- Purpose: 
This SOP aims to define the process for collecting and verifying data for new clients and 
demographic updates for existing groups, ensuring accurate and timely information for 
credentialing. It outlines steps for data collection, verification, follow-ups, and final 
uploading.  
2- Scope: 
This SOP outlines the standardized process for collecting, verifying, and uploading critical 
data for new clients and demographic updates within the credentialing department. Its 
purpose is to ensure that anyone who reads this SOP understands how information is 
gathered, verified, and utilized to maintain compliance, accuracy, and integrity in the 
credentialing process. This procedure applies to both new client onboardings and updates to 
existing group data, detailing the steps for data collection, document verification, 
communication with the Account Manager or provider, and final data uploading to relevant 
platforms.  
3- References: 
The International Standard ISO 9001:2015.

4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1-Abbreviations:   
• AIL 
: Accepted insurance list  
• AM 
: Account Manager  
• CAQH 
: Council for Affordable Quality Healthcare  
• CD 
: Credentialing Department  
• CEA 
: Credentialing External Accounts  
• CM 
: Credentialing Manager  
• CS  
: Credentialing Specialist  
• CMS 
: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services   
• EIN 
: Employer Identification Number  
• IRS 
: Internal Revenue Service  
• MEP 
: Medicaid Enrolled Provider  
• MMIS# : Medicaid Management Information System Number  
• NPI 
: National Provider Identifier  
• Pecos 
: Provider Enrollment, Chain, and Ownership System  
• PLI 
: Professional Liability Insurance  
• PTAN 
: Provider Transaction Access Number  
• SDD 
: Service Delivery Department  
• SSN 
: Social Security Number  
• OHEC 
: Operations Hub-External Clients  
4.2- Terms and definitions:    
- Accepted insurance list: A list that contains the insurances the provider and the 
enrolled with.  
- CAQH: A platform where healthcare providers can enter and maintain their 
professional and practice information, such as work experience, practice addresses, 
and education that helps ensure that all their credentials are up to date and health 
plans can access this information for the credentialing process.

- Certificate of incorporation: A registration with the government to operate legally 
and provided after successful governmental registration and it’s not a "certification" 
but is a legal document confirming the approving and existence of the company.  
- Clickup: A platform used by the CD to help manage tasks, projects, and workflows.  
- CMS / Pecos: CMS is a federal agency that administers Medicare healthcare 
program, and Pecos is a web-based platform managed by CMS that facilitates the 
enrollment process for Medicare providers and suppliers, allowing them to submit 
and manage their Medicare enrollment information electronically.  
- Credentialing External Accounts: A space on Clickup used by CD that has all 
external client files. 
- Credentialing Plan: A subsheet in the login sheet used to specify which rendering 
provider should be billed at which servicing location.  
- Data Collection: The process of collecting and verifying the data provided by the 
provider or AM in the onboarding stage or any other stages.  
- Demographic Update: A process of updating the provider and/or group information 
with insurance companies or adding a new provider under the group.  
- EIN: It’s a unique SSN for the business assigned by the government and used for tax 
purposes.  
- Google Drive File: A file created by the CM for each client using their group’s name 
and is used by the CS to store the provider/group documents.  
- IRS letter: It’s a letter from the federal department responsible for tax collection in 
the U.S. that verifies the Business Legal Name and Tax ID for the business.  
- License lookup: A lookup used to verify a provider’s license information. 
(Appendix 2)

- Login Sheet: Sheets for each client that includes all the information used by any 
related party working on this account to help the daily process, created and edited by 
the credentialing team.  
- MEP Lookup: A lookup used to get the MMIS# of enrolled providers. (Appendix 3)  
- MMIS#: The unique Medicaid identification number.  
- NPI: It’s a unique identifying number for any healthcare provider or group and 
organization used in the U.S.  
- NPPES lookup: A lookup used to verify the provider and/or group NPI.  
(Appendix 4)  
- Onboarding client: The process of integrating a new client into COB Solution’s 
system. 
- Onboarding/Expansion: A list in the OHEC used for the onboarding of clients and 
demographic update announcements.   
- Operations Hub-External Clients (OHEC): A space on Clickup used by CD and 
SDD for communication.  
- PTAN: The unique Medicare identification number.  
- PLI: It’s a shield for doctors. If a patient sues them for a mistake (malpractice), the 
insurance helps cover the costs. This gives doctors peace of mind to focus on their 
patients.  
- Preferences: This refers to the insurance plans the provider prefers to enroll in.  
- Proof of address: A document that proves the utilization of an address like a lease 
agreement, utility bill, insurance policy documents, etc.  
- Registration License: It’s a license provided by the state that permits the provider to 
practice in this state.

- Servicing location: Clinic’s location where the healthcare services are provided to 
the patients.  
- SSN card: A physical card issued by the Social Security Administration (SSA) in the 
U.S. that contains a unique nine-digit number used for identification, employment, 
and tax purposes.  
- State License: It’s a certification to ensure that all practicing physicians have 
appropriate education and training, and that they abide by professional standards 
while serving their patients.  
- Zip Code lookup: A lookup used to verify addresses.   
- Void Check: It's an unusable check that verifies the information of the company’s 
bank account and it should include (Account Holder Name ‘Business Legal Name’, 
Billing Address, Bank Name, Routing Number, Account Number).  
- W9 form: It’s a document to verify information for the group for tax payment 
purposes and it includes ‘Name of group or individual under group, Type of the 
Organization, Address, Special number, Signature, Date’.  
- Worker’s Compensation#: A unique identifier assigned to healthcare providers who 
treat patients under workers' compensation claims.  
- Worker’s Compensation# lookup: A lookup used to verify or get the Worker’s 
Compensation#.   
 
5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation: 
Title 
Responsibility 
Senior 
Credentialing 
Specialist (SCS) 
- Assigns a Credentialing Specialist (CS) to new clients. 
- Creates and maintains necessary documentation, such as Login 
Sheets and Google Drive files, for the onboarding process.  
- Updates the system (SDD) once the client is fully onboarded.  
- Ensures the credentialing process begins according to the 
provider’s preferences after data is verified.

6- Procedures: 
6.1- Data Collection and Verification for New Client:   
6.1.1 Announcement:  
- SDD announces the new client through an email called the onboarding email.  
- The group owner and each provider under the group fills click up forms.  
- Individual and group forms reflect as ‘To do’ tasks on the Onboarding Group Form 
code (PS/CRE-F-01) and Onboarding Provider Form (PS/CRE-F-02) list. SCS 
assigns a CS to the new client.  SCS duplicates a ‘New Account Template’ file on 
the ‘Credentialing External Accounts’ space on Clickup and names it with the 
group’s name.   
- SCS creates the Login Sheet (PS/CRE-F-05) for the onboarding client and creates a 
Google Drive file with the group’s name.   
6.1.2 Data collection:  
- CS collects data from the click up forms code (PS/CRE-F-01 & PS/CRE-F-02) and 
onboarding email in the new file on Click Up and New group’s new folder on the 
Drive. 
6.1.3 Data verification:   
- CS takes the collected data and verifies them through different methods check 
below:   
* Group Data:  
Data 
Verification Method 
Group Name 
Verified through the IRS letter 
EIN 
Verified through the IRS letter 
NPI 
CS verifies NPI number, specialty and Group’s name through 
the NPPES lookup 
Servicing Address 
Verified through the IRS letter first then the Zip lookup 
Billing Address 
Verified through the voided check 
Group PTAN 
(if applicable) 
Verified through Pecos 
Group MMIS# 
(if applicable) 
Verified through the MEP Lookup

6.1.4 Follow up:   
* In case communication with the provider is not approved:  
- CS creates a task on the demands and requests list on the Operations Hub- External 
Clients space. 
- Writes the missing information needed in the description.   
- Assigns the AM responsible for the new client and sets a due date.   
- Follows up with AM until the missing information/documents are provided.   
* In case communication is established:   
- CS follows up with the provider via email to get the missing information.   
6.1.5 Data Uploading:   
* Clickup:   
- CS fills the information on the group and provider files (PS/CRE-F-03 and 
PS/CRE-F-04) on the ‘CEA’ space  
- CS reflects the AIL on the location file on the ‘CEA’ space  
* Login Sheet:   
- CS fills the login sheet (PS/CRE-F-05) with the group and provider/s information.   
- CS reflects the AIL on the accepted insurances and credentialing plan sub sheets on 
the login sheet.  
* Google Drive:   
- SCS creates a folder for the Client and names it with the group name and then CS 
creates sub files in the created Group Drive Folder for the group and for each 
provider.   
- CS Uploads the group documents in the group subfile and names them Document X 
/ Group Name.   
- CS uploads the provider documents in the provider subfile and names them 
Document Y / Provider’s name.  
* Once all data and requirements are verified and agreed upon: 
- CS informs SCS that the client is fully onboarded   
- SCS updates SDD   
- The credentialing process starts based on the provider’s preferences.

6.2- Data Collection and verification for Demographic Update: 
6.2.1 Changes in existing information:  
These changes include but are not limited to: adding or updating servicing address/es, 
updating billing address, office phone, fax or email address, working hours, and/or 
changing provider’s name.   
* Announcement:   
- AM announces the demographic update via email.   
- AM creates a task on the Onboarding/Expansion list on the Operations Hub- 
External Clients space on clickup with the new information.   
* Data Collection:  
- CS confirms receipt of the task and collects the new information provided.   
* Data verification:   
- CS takes the collected data and verifies them through different methods check 
below:  
Data 
Verification Method 
Servicing Address 
Verified through the Zip Code lookup or in some cases with 
the AM 
Billing Address 
Verified through the voided check 
Office phone and 
fax numbers 
Verified with the AM 
Working hours 
Verified with the AM 
Void check 
CS verifies the document is supplied, then verifies the billing 
address on the VC and the word Void is written on it 
W9 form 
CS verifies the document is supplied and then verifies the 
group name, EIN, billing address and provider’s signature 
Existing Provider’s 
Name 
Verified through the Resume, NPPES lookup, and license 
lookup 
- If any of these data and/or documents are missing, CS reaches out to AM to provide 
the missing data and/or documents or to ask for communication approval with the 
provider.  
- If communication is approved, CS emails the provider asking for the requested 
information.

and verifies them through different methods check below:

1- Purpose: 
This SOP defines the standardized process for collecting, verifying, and uploading critical 
data for new Physical Therapy providers joining PT of The City (PTOC). It aims to ensure 
accurate and timely information for credentialing, system setup, and compliance, outlining 
steps for data collection, verification, follow-ups, and final uploading to relevant platforms. 
 
2- Scope: 
This SOP applies to all Credentialing Staff and cross-departmental teams (e.g., Staffing, 
Executive Management) involved in the onboarding of new PTOC providers for clinic 
assignments or home therapy services. It covers the process from receipt of the hiring 
clearance through to the provider's data being fully integrated into all required systems. 
 
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
3.2- Internal "Credentialing Manual" Sheet.

4- Terms / Terminology:   
4.1- Abbreviation: 
4.1.1- CAQH: Council for Affordable Quality Healthcare 
4.1.2- CMS : Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services 
4.1.3- CS 
: Credentialing Specialist 
4.1.4- MEP : Medicaid Enrolled Provider  
4.1.5- MMIS#: Medicaid Management Information System Number 
4.1.6- NPI 
: National Provider Identifier 
4.1.7- Pecos : Provider Enrollment, Chain, and Ownership System 
4.1.8- PTOC : Physical Therapy of The City 
4.1.9- SSN 
: Social Security Number 
4.1.10- USPS : United States Postal Services 
4.1.11- WC : Worker’s Compensation 
 4.2- Terms: 
4.2.1- CAQH: is a platform where healthcare providers can enter and maintain their 
professional and practice information, such as work experience, practice addresses, 
and education that helps ensure that all their credentials are up to date and health 
plans can access this information for the credentialing process. 
4.2.2- ClickUp: a platform used by the Credentialing department to help manage tasks, 
projects, and workflows. 
4.2.3- Credentialing Plan: A sheet where all PTOC locations are added in sub-sheets is 
used to specify which rendering provider is at which servicing location. 
4.2.4- Data Collection: is the process of collecting and verifying the data provided by the 
provider in the onboarding stage. 
4.2.5- Demographic Update: a process of updating the provider and/or group information 
with insurance companies, adding a new service location under the group, or adding 
a location to a provider under the group. 
4.2.6- Google Drive File: A file created by the CS for each provider under PTOC is used 
by the CS to store the provider documents.

4.2.7- Hiring Clearance email: Hiring Clearance is an email received from the staffing 
department confirming that a new provider has completed the onboarding process 
and is cleared to begin working. It includes relevant details about the provider's 
status with all required data and documents to begin and readiness to start providing 
services. 
4.2.8- Home Therapy: PT Providers under PT of The City Group offer personalized 
physical therapy services to patients in their homes. These providers focus on 
delivering convenient, in-home care tailored to individual patient needs. 
4.2.9- Location list: a list on the PTOC space on ClickUp that has the tasks of a 
provider/group to document and track updates and information of providers/groups. 
4.2.10- Login Sheet: It’s a Google sheet for PTOC that includes all the information used 
by any related party working on PTOC to help the daily process. 
4.2.11- Malpractice: It’s a shield for doctors. If a patient sues them for a mistake, the 
insurance helps cover the costs. This gives doctors peace of mind to focus on their 
patients. 
4.2.12- MEP Lookup: A lookup is used to get the MMIS# of enrolled providers.  
4.2.13- NPI: It’s a unique identifying number for any healthcare provider or group and 
organization used in the U.S. 
4.2.14- NPPES lookup: A lookup used to verify the provider and/or group NPI.  
4.2.15- PTOC Agenda: a google sheet for internal departmental use that tracks provider 
information, credentialing process of all providers, and contains email templates 
and location details. 
4.2.16- Registration License: It’s a license provided by the state that permits the provider 
to practice in this state. 
4.2.17- Rendering provider: the provider that is providing the services. 
4.2.18- Servicing location: Clinic’s location where the healthcare services are provided to 
the patients. 
4.2.19- SSN Card: is a physical card issued by the Social Security Administration (SSA) 
in the U.S. that contains a unique nine-digit number used for identification, 
employment, and tax purposes.

4.2.20- State License: It’s a certification to ensure that all practicing physicians have 
appropriate education and training and that they abide by professional standards 
while serving their patients.  
4.2.21- Therabill: is a billing system designed for healthcare providers, particularly in the 
behavioral health field. It helps manage patient billing, insurance claims, and 
payment tracking, making the billing process easier and more efficient for practices. 
4.2.22- USPS lookup: A lookup that helps you find the postal code (Zip Code) for any 
address in the United States. It allows you to enter an address, and it will provide 
the correct Zip Code for that location and correct it.  
4.2.23- Worker’s Compensation#: is a unique identifier assigned to healthcare providers 
who treat patients under workers' compensation claims.  
5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation: 
Title 
Responsibility 
Senior Credentialing 
Specialist (SCS) 
- Responsible for the overall oversight of the procedure, 
assigning tasks, and ensuring compliance. 
Credentialing 
Specialist (CS) 
- Responsible for executing the data collection, verification, 
follow-up, and uploading steps as outlined in this procedure. 
PTOC HR Team 
- Responsible for triggering the process by sending the 
complete "Hiring Clearance" task to the Credentialing team. 
 
6- Procedures: 
6.1- Data Collection and Verification for New PTOC Provider: 
6.1.1- Announcement: 
- The HR team announces the new provider by submitting the "Hiring Clearance" 
task to the Credentialing team via Clickup (PS/CRE-F-06).  
- The task must include: 
- Provider's full name and assigned location (clinic or home therapy). 
- All required documents: Updated resume, active malpractice insurance (PLI), 
state license, registration license, SSN, date of birth, and professional photo (for 
ZocDoc). 
- The SCS assigns the responsible CS to the task.

6.1.4- Verified Data Uploading:  
- CS uploads the verified data on the ‘PTOC Agenda’ sheet on the ‘PTOC Providers’ 
sub sheet. (Appendix 5) 
6.2- Credentialed PTs for Home Therapy Only: 
6.2.1- Announcement & Initiation: 
- Receipt of the "Hiring Clearance" email from the Staffing Department, which must 
specify the provider's designation as "Home Therapy". 
- The assigned CS confirms receipt and reviews the email.  
6.2.2- Data Collection & Folder Setup: 
- The CS creates a dedicated provider folder in the PTOC Google Drive. 
- A subfolder is created named [Provider First Name Last Name - Home Therapy]. 
- 
All 
provided 
documents 
are 
uploaded 
as 
PDFs 
with 
standardized 
filenames: [Document Type] / [Provider First Name Last Name]. 
6.2.3- Data Verification: 
- The CS verifies all individual provider. The verification criteria are identical to 
Section 6.1.3 for clinic providers. 
- The "Servicing Location" is administratively set as "Home Visits Therapy - 
PTOC" with the address of "Mid-town - PTOC HeadQuarter".  
- This is a system requirement and does not require physical address verification. 
6.2.4- Data Uploading & System Integration (Home Therapy Specific): 
- The provider is added to ClickUp under the "Home Visits Therapy - PTOC" list. 
- The Servicing Location must be set to: "Home Visits Therapy - PTOC (Mid-town - 
PTOC HeadQuarter)". 
- All other verified data (Name, DOB, SSN, NPI, etc.) is entered as standard. 
- The PTOC Agenda Sheet is updated with the new provider’s information. 
- The "Servicing Location" column must clearly state "Home Therapy".

- CS ensures all provider documents are uploaded in the [Provider First Name_Last 
Name - Home Therapy] subfolder. 
- HOME THERAPY PROVIDERS ARE NOT ADDED TO ZOCDOC. This step is 
explicitly skipped. No action is required. 
6.2.5- Completion: 
- Once all data and requirements are verified and uploaded to the specified systems, 
the home therapy provider is considered fully onboarded, and the credentialing 
process begins. 
6.3- Adding a New Servicing Location/Clinic: 
6.3.1- Announcement: 
- Obtain a formal email from the Executive/Technical Manager confirming the new 
service location, including:  
- Full address.  
- Operational start date.  
- Name of the service location/clinic. 
- USPS Address Validation: 
- Verify the address using the USPS Address Validation Tool.  
- Invalid Address: Collaborate with the Credentialing Lead, Executive, and Technical 
Manager that the address is not correct and take the proper action  
- Valid Address: Proceed to database updates.  
6.3.2- Data Collection & Verification: 
- CS confirms receipt of the announcement email. 
- Servicing Address: Verified using the USPS Zip Code Lookup tool. 
- Invalid Address: CS escalates to the Credentialing Lead, who collaborates with 
the Executive and Technical Manager to resolve. 
- Valid Address: Proceed to system updates. 
6.3.3- System Integration: 
- PT of the City Login Sheet: 
-  CS adds the new address to the "Service Locations" section. 
- ClickUp:  
- CS creates a folder labeled with the location name, generates a specific list, and 
assigns tasks to the responsible CS. 
- Therabill:  
- CS adds the new service location to Therabill once the operational date is 
confirmed.

1- Purpose: 
The objective of this procedure is to establish a clear and standardized method for processing 
demographic updates and adding new providers to groups. This process aims to ensure 
accuracy, timely completion, and consistent tracking of changes, maintaining compliance 
with the credentialing requirements.  
2- Scope: 
This procedure outlines the standardized process for collecting, verifying, and processing 
demographic updates for existing providers or adding new providers to a group within the 
credentialing department. Its purpose is to ensure that all team members follow a consistent 
approach for gathering, verifying, and submitting provider demographic information to 
maintain accuracy, compliance, and integrity in the credentialing process.  
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
3.2- Credentialing Manual

4.2 Terms and Definitions:   
4.2.1- Credentialing External Accounts: A space on ClickUp used by CD that has all 
external client files.   
4.2.2- Credentialing Manual: A detailed manual used by the CD for enrollment, 
demographic updates, EFT/ERA/EDI setup, and contact information for each 
insurance.  
4.2.3- INN Email Thread: An email thread created by the CM to notify the B&A 
department, SDD, about the new enrollment/ demographic updates of a group or 
provider.  
4.2.4- Location List: A list on the CEA space on ClickUp that has the tasks of a 
provider/group to document and track updates and information of providers/groups.  
4.2.5- Network Status: The status that indicates a provider is INN or OON.  
4.2.6- Onboarding/Expansion: A list in the OHEC used for the onboarding of clients and 
demographic update announcements  
4.2.7- Operations Hub-External Clients: A space on ClickUp used by CD and SDD for 
communication.

- Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are 
integrated within business processes. 
Account Manager 
(AM) 
- Announces new providers via email.  
- Creates tasks in ClickUp for new provider additions or 
demographic updates.  
Credentialing 
Specialist (CS) 
- Collects and verifies demographic data as per the Data 
Collection and Verification procedure.  
- Proceeds with demographic updates based on the provider’s 
network status (INN or OON).  
- Submits necessary forms via website, email, fax, mail, or 
portal as per credentialing guidelines.   
- Follows up with insurers to ensure demographic updates or 
new provider additions are completed.  
- Documents actions in ClickUp and updates the login sheet.  
- Escalates tasks after multiple follow-ups and seeks alternative 
provider relations contacts when necessary. 
Senior 
Credentialing 
Specialist (SCS) 
- Provides guidance on complex cases. 
- Creates and manages the monthly INN email thread for 
updates. 
- Maintains the Credentialing Manual and template library. 
Provider 
- Promptly signs and returns any necessary forms. 
- Provides accurate and complete demographic information and 
required documents in a timely manner.

▪ If website submission:

form.

ClickUp using a template (code: PS/CRE-F-07).   
• Assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status to ‘Demo update in 
process’ and sets a due date.   
▪ If Email Submission:   
• CS checks credentialing manual for steps, form/s and insurance email 
address.   
• Fills the form and reviews the inserted data.

the required documents.   
• Sends the enrollment email with the subject ‘Demographic Update Request 
- {Group’s Name}’.   
• Documents the action taken on the ‘location list’ on the ‘CEA’ space on 
ClickUp using a template (code: PS/CRE-F-07).  
• Assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status to ‘Demo update in 
process’ and sets a due date.

again.  
• If the forms are rejected:

corrected documents. 
▪ CS continues to follow up until the new provider is added under the 
group.

1- Purpose: 
This procedure aims to establish a standardized and efficient process for credentialing 
specialists to set up Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) and Electronic Remittance Advice 
(ERA) for healthcare providers, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and timeliness in the 
processing of the enrollments. 
2- Scope: 
2.1-This procedure applies to all credentialing specialists responsible for EDI and ERA setup. 
It outlines the entire process, including: 
• Receiving the setup requests 
• Submitting enrollments 
• Monitoring enrollment status 
• Ensuring accurate Completion of the Enrollments 
2.2- By following this procedure, credentialing specialists can maintain consistency, reduce 
errors, and improve the efficiency of the EDI & ERA Setup. 
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
3.2- Credentialing Training Manual 
3.3- Procedures for Data Collection 
3.4- Medicare (NGS) and Medicaid (ETIN) enrollment regulations

4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1-Abbreviation:  
4.1.1- AM 
: Account Manager 
4.1.2- B&AD : Billing & Authorization Department 
4.1.3- CS 
: Credentialing Specialist 
4.1.4- EDI 
: Electronic Data Interchange 
4.1.5- EEL : EDI Enrollments List (tracked in ClickUp) 
4.1.6- (E&PS): EDIs and Portals Space (ClickUp workspace) 
4.1.7- EOB : explanation of benefits 
4.1.8- ERA : Electronic Remittance Advice 
4.1.9- ETIN : Electronic Transmitter Identification Number 
4.1.10- NGS : National Government Services 
4.1.11- PID : Packet ID 
4.1.12- SDD : Service Delivery Department 
4.2-Terms and Definitions: 
4.2.1- Electronic Data Interchange (EDI): It is a process that allows the submission of 
electronic claims to insurance companies. 
4.2.2- Electronic Remittance Advice (ERA): It is the electronic version of paper EOBs 
and provides details about claim payments. 
4.2.3- ERA &EDI enrollment: the process of linking the billing software to its integrated 
clearinghouse in order to submit claims and receive the electronic responses 
4.2.4- Electronic Transmitter Identification Number (ETIN): It’s a four-digit billing 
code that allows providers to electronically submit claims and check eligibility for 
Medicaid in New York. 
4.2.5- Packet ID (PID): A unique identification number assigned to a packet of 
enrollment information submitted through the NGS Medicare Enrollment Portal. 
4.2.6- National Government Services (NGS): A Medicare Administrative Contractor 
(MAC) that processes Medicare claims and provides other services to healthcare 
providers. 
4.2.7- ClickUp: Project management platform used for task submission, status updates, 
and logging issues.

6- Procedures: 
6.1- ERA Setup Procedure: 
6.1.1- Receive the ERA setup request: 
• The credentialing specialist (CS) receives a request from the (B&AD) or (SDD) 
after the provider’s insurance enrollment is completed. 
• In this task the CS should be provided by the Payer Name and Payer ID from SDD 
or B&A department (the department who creates the task) and the task is reflected in 
the (EEL) in the EDIs and Portals Space on ClickUp. 
• CS assigns a due date for this task to ensure it is not forgotten. 
6.1.2- Identifying the Billing System: 
• The credentialing specialist (CS) confirms the provider's preferred billing system for 
ERA submission with the account manager (AM) 
• The process varies based on the type of billing system: 
• If the provider requires a specific billing system (one account for one group, the 
Account Manager (AM) is responsible for setting up the system for that provider. 
• If the provider will be using an existing, common billing system (used by multiple 
groups): The Account Manager (AM) updates the billing system by adding the 
provider’s Tax ID. 
• Credentialing Specialist (CS) updates the EDI Enrollments folder name of the group 
to accurately reflect the provider’s designated billing system. 
6.1.3- ERA Enrollment Submission: 
• CS retrieves login credentials for the billing system from the credentials sub-sheet in 
the manual or directly from the login sheet (Code: PS/CRE-F-05) for each group, 
depending on the billing system used. 
• CS accesses the ERA Enrollment section within the billing system. Depending on 
the system, this process may vary:  
▪ In Billing systems, CS may need to enter the group name and then search using:  
Payer ID or Payer Name and navigate to the ERA enrollment Section.

▪ In other systems, no need to enter the group name as the account is already linked 
to the Tax ID of the group. 
• CS follows the specific ERA enrollment steps for the billing system used. And 
enters the required information of the group that was collected in the Data 
Collection Step (referencing the procedure of Data Collection as a guide). 
• CS submits the ERA, and the method of submission depends on the billing system: 
▪ Direct Enrollment (If Applicable): Some billing systems allow for direct ERA 
enrollment within the system itself, and CS follows those specific steps. 
▪ Via Email: Some Billing systems may require an ERA enrollment submission via 
email. 
• CS checks if the email address for the enrollment submission is listed in the Manual 
under EDI/ERA/EFT section.  
• If the email is not updated, CS updates the manual with the correct address provided 
in the form obtained from the billing system.  
• CS composes an email with the following details:  
o Subject: ERA Enrollment Request 
o Body includes: 
▪ Provider Information 
▪ Attaches ERA enrollment Form (if applicable) 
▪ Request for confirmation of receipt 
6.1.4- Update of ClickUp: 
• CS updates the task in the EDI EEL on ClickUp to track all interactions with the 
clearinghouse /Practice Mate and ensures a complete record of the process. The 
updates should include: 
o Status of the task updated to "In Process" 
o Date of the ERA setup Submission 
o Accurate comment on the update and the Submission Method (Via the Billing 
system Portal or email) 
o Submission Ticket number (If applicable) 
o Email address and Email subject (For Emails)

Page 7 of 10 
6.1.5- ERA Enrollment Follow-Up Process: 
• If ERA enrollment is submitted through the Billing System: CS logs into the billing 
system to check the enrollment status. 
• The status could be: 
o Pending: The enrollment is still being processed. 
o Payer Received: The payer has received the enrollment. 
• If there is any delay or the status is not progressing, the CS will submit a support 
ticket to investigate if additional actions are needed from the credentialing team. 
• If ERA Enrollment is submitted through Email: Follow up by sending an email to 
the designated email address to inquire about the enrollment status. 
• Document the Follow-Up process: 
o CS documents all follow-up dates (when the follow-up occurred). 
o CS records the method of follow-up (whether it was through the billing system or 
email). 
o CS updates the Task in ClickUp with the next action to be taken, ensuring that the 
next steps are clear for further follow-up. 
6.1.6- Completion of ERA Setup: 
• The CS confirms that the EDI & ERA setup is complete using one of the following 
methods: 
o Billing System Status Update: The CS checks the billing system, where the status 
will change to "Payer Approved", confirming that the setup is complete. 
o Provider Notification: The insurance provider notifies the provider about the 
completed setup. The provider then forwards this confirmation to the 
credentialing department. 
o Direct Notification:  The insurance provider directly informs the credentialing 
department that the setup is complete. 
• Once the enrollment is confirmed as completed, the CS takes the following actions: 
o The CS records the effective date of the ERA enrollment in the EDI Enrollments 
List on ClickUp marks the task as Complete in ClickUp to indicate the process is 
finalized. 
o Updates the EFT & ERA sub-sheet within the login sheet (code: PS/CRE-F-13)

6.2- EDI Setup:  CS submits the EDI enrollment as part of the provider’s insurance 
enrollment process. 
6.2.1- Medicaid EDI Enrollment Process: 
• CS fills out the ETIN enrollment form (Code: PS/CRE-F-11) with the 
group/provider information for the Medicaid enrollment. 
• Sends the completed form to the provider via email for their signature and 
notarization. Note: Electronic notarization is not accepted. 
• Instructs the provider to submit the notarized document via mail. The provider also 
shares the tracking number with CS for the follow-up process. 
• Updates the tracking number in the ETIN list within the assigned group folder for 
tracking purposes. 
• Once the enrollment with Medicaid is completed, the CS ensures that the ETIN is 
activated and that the four-digit ETIN is provided and recorded in the ETIN list. 
• CS updates the effective date and expiration date of the ETIN in the ETIN list. 
• Before the ETIN expiration date, the CS sends an ETIN recertification form (Code 
PS/CRE-F-12) to the provider and follows the same submission instructions for this 
process. 
• All the steps should be documented on ClickUp with the date of each process and 
the update. 
6.2.2- Medicare EDI Enrollment process: 
• After the provider enrollment is completed, CS logs into the Billing System to begin 
the EDI enrollment process. 
• The CS accesses the NGS Medicare Link to initiate the enrollment process for the 
provider. 
• The CS enters the group information and clearinghouse (or Practice Mate) details 
into the system. 
• After entering the required information and Submission of the EDI Enrollment, the 
system generates a Packet ID (PID), for the tracking of the enrollment. 
• CS then updates EDI Task in EDI enrollment list specific to the group under the EDI 
and Portal Space in ClickUp, with the date of the submission and Packet ID for 
reference.

1- Purpose: 
This procedure aims to establish a standardized and efficient process for credentialing 
specialists to set up Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) for healthcare providers, ensuring 
accuracy and timeliness in receiving payments.  
2- Scope: 
2.1- This procedure applies to all credentialing specialists involved in setting up EFT for 
healthcare providers. 
2.2- It covers the entire EFT setup lifecycle, from receiving the initial request and collecting 
data, through enrollment submission, follow-up, and final confirmation, including the 
handling of any related issues. 
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
3.2- Internal "Credentialing Manual" Sheet. 
3.3- Procedure: Data Collection and Verification for PTOC Providers 
3.4- Procedure: Portal Creation

4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- Abbreviations: 
4.1.1- AM 
: Account Manager 
4.1.2- B&AD : Billing and Authorization Department 
4.1.3- CS 
: Credentialing Specialist 
4.1.4- EFT : Electronic Funds Transfer 
4.1.5- ERA : Electronic Remittance Advice 
4.1.6- EDI 
: Electronic Data Interchange 
4.1.7- IBVW : Information of the Group, Billing Address, Voided Check, W9 
4.1.8- NPI 
: National Provider Identifier 
4.1.9- NPPES: National Plan and Provider Enumeration System 
4.1.10- SDD : Service Delivery Department 
4.2- Definitions: 
4.2.1- Clearinghouse: A medical intermediary that enables the exchange of healthcare 
data (e.g., EFT enrollments) between the provider and the payer (insurance 
company). 
4.2.2- Credentialing Manual: A detailed internal manual used by the credentialing 
department for enrollment, demographic updates, and EFT/ERA/EDI setup 
processes. 
4.2.3- Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT): A method for moving money electronically 
between bank accounts. It connects a provider’s bank account directly with 
the insurance company’s account for direct payment of claims. 
4.2.4- Void Check: An unusable check that verifies the company's bank account 
information. It must include the account holder name (business legal name), 
billing address, bank name, routing number, account number, and be clearly 
marked "VOID".

Responsibility

- Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this

- Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements

- Responsible for the overall oversight of the EFT procedure, 
ensuring compliance, and resolving escalations. 
Credentialing Specialist 
(CS) 
- Responsible for executing the EFT setup process from 
initiation to completion, including data collection, 
enrollment, follow-up, and documentation. 
Billing & Authorization 
Dept (B&AD) / Service 
Delivery Dept (SDD) 
- Responsible for initiating the EFT setup process by 
submitting a task in ClickUp after insurance enrollment is 
complete. 
 
6- Procedures: 
6.1- Task Initiation and Data Preparation 
6.1.1- Announcement: 
• Trigger: The credentialing specialist (CS) receives a task in the "EDI Enrollments 
List" within the "EDIs and Portals" space on ClickUp from B&AD or SDD. 
• Action: The CS sets a due date for the task to ensure timely completion. 
6.1.2- Data Collection & Verification: 
• The CS collects and verifies the required information, referencing the "Data 
Collection" procedure where applicable: 
o Group/Individual Info: Tax ID, NPI (verified via NPPES lookup - Appendix 4), 
Group Name, Provider Name. 
o Service & Billing Address: Verified via USPS lookup (Appendix 6). 
o Void Check: CS verifies the document is supplied and contains all required 
elements: bank account/routing number, correct group name, billing address, and 
"VOID" mark. Confirms the account is linked to the group's tax ID.

• Handling Missing Data (IBVW): If any information of the group, billing address, 
voided check, or W9 is missing, the CS requests it from the AM and follows up until 
obtained. 
• System Update: The CS updates ClickUp and the designated Google Drive with all 
collected data and documents. 
6.2- EFT Enrollment Submission 
6.2.1- Determine Submission Method: 
• The CS contacts the insurance provider to determine the EFT setup method: Direct 
Enrollment or via a clearinghouse. 
6.2.2- Execute Enrollment: 
• The CS follows the method-specific steps below, referencing the internal credentialing 
manual and the "Portal Creation" procedure. 
Submission Method 
CS Action 
Direct 
(Portal/Website) 
1. Access the portal/website following the steps in the manual. 
2. Navigate to EFT section. 
3. Complete the form with IBVW data. 
4. Submit and note the ticket/reference number. 
Direct (Email) 
1. Send email to designated address in the manual. 
2. Include provider info (Group Name, Tax ID, NPI, Address) and 
list attachments. 
3. Request confirmation. 
Via Clearinghouse 
1. Log in to clearinghouse portal (use credentials from Login Sheet 
of the client). 
2. Navigate to EFT section. 
3. Complete the form with IBVW data. 
4. Submit. Ensure status reaches "Payer Received". 
Note: EFT enrollments require payer-specific digital form submissions. These forms are 
external and controlled by the respective payer, not by Practice Solution. 
• Issue Resolution: If any issues arise during submission, the CS contacts the respective 
technical support (portal, website, or clearinghouse) to resolve them before re-
submitting.

6.2.3- Task Documentation: 
• The CS immediately updates the ClickUp task with: 
o Status: "In Process" 
o Submission Date, Method, and Ticket/Reference Number 
o Representative name (if called) or Email Subject 
6.3- Follow-up and Completion 
6.3.1- Follow-up Process: 
• The CS follows up based on the submission method to confirm receipt and check 
status. 
o Direct/Portal/Email: Follow up via phone or email, referencing the ticket ID. 
o Clearinghouse: Log in to check status; contact clearinghouse support if delayed; 
confirm with insurance that they received the enrollment. 
• Documentation: The CS documents all follow-up actions in the ClickUp task (date, 
method, contact, status, next steps). 
6.3.2- Handling missing information identified during follow-up: 
• If the insurance requests additional data, the CS obtains it, submits it via the requested 
method (email/fax), and follows up to confirm receipt. 
6.3.3- Completion: 
• Confirmation: The CS confirms EFT setup is complete via one of: direct confirmation 
from insurance, "Payer Approved" status in a portal, or official notification. 
• Final Documentation: The CS obtains the effective date, marks the ClickUp task as 
"Complete", and updates the EFT & ERA sub-sheet in the group's Login Sheet (code: 
PS/CRE-F-13). 
6.4- Handling Post-Setup Issues 
6.4.1- Issue Intake: 
• Trigger: B&AD or SDD submits a task in the "Demands and Requests" or "Billing 
Forms" space on ClickUp regarding an EFT issue. 
• Action: The CS investigates the issue by contacting the insurance provider. 
6.4.2- Resolution: 
• The CS takes corrective action, which may include requesting additional info from the 
provider or coordinating with the insurance/clearinghouse. 
• The CS thoroughly documents the issue, actions taken, and final resolution in the 
ClickUp task.

1- Purpose: 
The objective of this procedure is to outline the standardized process for enrolling providers 
into insurance networks, both in-network (INN) and out-of-network (OON). This procedure 
ensures the efficient and accurate completion of the provider enrollment process, including 
data collection, verification, submission, follow-up, and documentation. It aims to maintain 
compliance, accuracy, and ensure that all enrollments are processed in a timely and organized 
manner. 
2- Scope: 
This procedure is for Credentialing Specialists (CS) and details the steps to submit enrollment 
applications for healthcare providers and groups into insurance networks (both INN and 
OON). This procedure includes determining the enrollment method (website, email, fax, or 
mail), submitting forms and following up on submissions, handling rejections or missing 
forms, and updating platforms like ClickUp and the AIL subsheet. The scope covers all 
stages from initial data collection to enrollment confirmation and final updates 
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
3.2- Data Collection and Verification Procedure 
3.3- Credentialing Manual 
3.4- Portal Creation and Management Procedure

4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- Abbreviations:  
4.1.1- AIL: Accepted Insurance List 
4.1.2- AM: Account Manager 
4.1.3- B&A: Billing and Authorization Department 
4.1.4- CD: Credentialing Department 
4.1.5- CEA: Credentialing External Accounts

4.1.7- CS: Credentialing Specialist 
4.1.8- INN: In-Network 
4.1.9- OON: Out-of-Network 
4.1.10- PR/S: Provider Relations/Services 
4.1.11- SCS: Senior Credentialing Specialist 
4.1.12- SDD: Service Delivery Department 
4.1.13- USPS: United States Postal Service 
4.2-Terms and Definition: 
4.2.1- Accepted Insurance List (AIL): A list that contains all the insurance plans the 
provider is enrolled with, including network status and effective dates. 
4.2.2- AIL Subsheet: A subsheet in the Login Sheet used to track the provider's insurance 
enrollments, indicating network status (INN or OON) and effective dates for each 
insurance. 
4.2.3- Credentialing External Accounts (CEA): A dedicated space on ClickUp used by 
the Credentialing Department to manage all external client files and tasks. 
4.2.4- Credentialing Manual: A comprehensive guide used by the Credentialing 
Department for enrollment processes, demographic updates, EFT/ERA/EDI setup, 
and insurance-specific contact information.  
4.2.5- Credentialing Plan: A subsheet in the Login Sheet used to specify which rendering 
provider should be billed at which servicing location (code: PS/CRE-F-14).

4.2.6- Effective Date: The date when insurance enrollment becomes active and the 
provider can begin seeing patients under that insurance plan. 
4.2.7- Escalation: The process of seeking assistance from senior staff or alternative 
contacts when standard follow-up methods fail to resolve an issue after multiple 
attempts (typically 4 follow-ups). 
4.2.8- INN Email Thread: A monthly email thread created by the Credentialing Manager 
to notify the Billing & Authorization Department and Service Delivery Department 
about new enrollments and demographic updates. 
4.2.9- Location List: A task list within the CEA space on ClickUp that tracks and 
documents all updates and information related to providers and groups. 
4.2.10- Login Sheet: A centralized spreadsheet for each client containing all provider 
information, insurance details, credentials, and access information used by the 
credentialing team and related parties. 
4.2.11- Network Preference: The provider's choice to participate as either In-Network 
(INN) or Out-of-Network (OON) with specific insurance payers. 
4.2.12- Network Status: Indicates whether a provider is In-Network (INN) or Out-of-
Network (OON) with a specific insurance plan. 
4.2.13- Portal: An online system used by insurance companies for electronic enrollment 
submissions, tracking, and management. 
4.2.14- Provider: A healthcare professional or entity being enrolled with insurance 
networks to provide medical services. 
4.2.15- Rendering Provider: The individual healthcare provider who directly delivers 
services to patients. 
4.2.16- Service Location: The physical address where healthcare services are provided to 
patients. 
4.2.17- USPS Tracking Lookup: An online tool used to track mailed packages and 
confirm delivery status. (Code: PS/CRE-A-07).

6- Procedures: 
6.1- Announcement is made and the data is collected and verified, check the Data Collection 
procedure  
6.2- CS proceeds with the enrollment based on the provider’s network preference (either INN 
or OON) 
6.2.1- In-network enrollment: 
• CS determines the enrollment submission method based on the steps on the 
Credentialing Manual.  
o  If website submission:  
▪  CS checks credentialing manual for steps and link.  
▪  Fills the form on the website and reviews the inserted data and submits the      
form.  
▪ Documents the action taken on the ‘location list’ on the ‘CEA’ space on ClickUp 
using a template (code: PS/CRE-F-07).  
▪ Assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status and sets a due date.  
o If Email Submission: 
▪ CS checks credentialing manual for steps, form/s and insurance email address.  
▪ CS fills the form and reviews the inserted data.  
▪ CS writes the email using a template from the email templates sheet (code: 
PS/CRE-F-07) and attaches the filled form/s along with the required documents.  
▪ CS sends the enrollment email with Subject: New Enrollment - {Group’s Name}.  
▪ CS documents the action taken on the ‘location list’ on the ‘CEA’ space on 
ClickUp using a template (code: PS/CRE-F-07).  
▪  CS assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status and sets a due date.  
o If fax submission:  
▪ CS checks credentialing manual for steps, form/s and insurance fax number.  
▪ CS fills the form and reviews the inserted data.  
▪ CS sends the enrollment fax with Subject: New Enrollment - {Group’s Name} 
attaching the filled form/s along with the required documents.  
▪ CS documents the action taken on the ‘location list’ on the ‘CEA’ space on 
ClickUp using a template (code: PS/CRE-F-07).  
▪ CS assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status and sets a due date.

6.2.2- Out-of-network enrollment:  
• CS checks the Credentialing Manual to determine if the enrollment requires a setup. If 
setup is needed, the submission method will follow the steps outlined in the manual.  
o If fax submission: 
▪ CS checks credentialing manual for steps, form/s and insurance fax number.  
▪ Fills and signs the form/s and reviews the inserted data.  
▪ Sends the enrollment fax with the subject ‘New OON Enrollment - {Group’s 
Name}’ attaching the filled form/s along with the required documents.  
▪ Documents the action taken on the ‘location list’ on the ‘CEA’ space on ClickUp 
using a template (code: PS/CRE-F-07). 
▪  Assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status and sets a due date.  
o If through portal:  
▪  CS checks credentialing manual for steps and the portal link. 
▪ Creates a portal (procedure portal creation). 
▪ If an account already exists, CS contacts AM to get credentials or approval to 
contact the provider and ask for the credentials. 
▪ CS signs in on the portal and fills the necessary information. 
▪ Submits the enrollment request. 
▪ Documents the action taken on the ‘location list’ on the ‘CEA’ space on ClickUp 
using a template (code: PS/CRE-F-07). 
▪ Assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status and sets a due date.

6.3- Follow up (depends on the enrollment method) 
6.3.1- INN enrollments follow up: 
o If website submission: 
▪ CS checks manual for the method of communication with the insurance. 
▪ CS calls or emails provider relations or services and provides either the reference 
number or the date they received the application. 
▪ CS documents the action taken on the task on ‘location list’ on the ‘CEA’ space 
following the template (code: PS/CRE-F-07). 
▪ If there are updates the CS changes the task status and the due date. 
▪ If there are no updates the CS changes the task due date, escalates after 4 follow-
ups; seeks alternate PR/S contacts for updates and asks for an updated contact 
then follow up till the enrollment is completed. 
o If email submission:  
▪ CS sends a follow-up email on the same request that was sent. 
▪ CS documents the action taken on the task on ‘location list’ on the ‘CEA’ space 
following the template 
▪ If there are updates the CS changes the task status and the due date.  
▪ If there are no updates the CS changes the task due date, escalates after 4 follow-
ups; seeks alternate PR/S contacts for updates and asks for an updated contact 
then follow up till the enrollment is completed. 
o If fax submission:  
▪ CS calls PR/S and provides the date of submission. 
▪ CS documents the action taken on the task on ‘location list’ on the ‘CEA’ space 
following the template. 
▪ If there are updates the CS changes the task status and the due date.  
▪ If there are no updates the CS changes the task due date, escalates after 4 follow-
ups; seeks alternate PR/S contacts for updates and asks for an updated contact 
then follow up till the enrollment is completed.

o If Mail submission:

▪ If pending, CS waits until form/s are delivered.  
▪ If form/s are returned, CS contacts the provider to mail them again.  
▪ If delivered, CS contacts the insurance again to confirm that they have received 
the document. 
▪ If no tracking no. was provided the CS follows with the insurance and if the time 
exceeded the standard processing period, the CS asks the provider to resend the 
document by mail.  
▪ CS documents the action taken on the task on ‘location list’ on the ‘CEA’ space 
following the template. 
▪ CS changes the task due date, escalates after 4 follow-ups; seeks alternate PR/S 
contacts for updates and asks for an updated contact then follow up till the 
enrollment is completed.

following the template (code: PS/CRE-F-07). 
▪ If there are updates the CS changes the task status and the due date.  
▪ If there are no updates the CS changes the task due date, escalates after 4 follow-
ups; seeks alternate PR/S contacts for updates and asks for an updated contact 
then follow up till the enrollment is completed. 
o If through portal:  
▪ CS opens the portal and checks the application. 
▪ CS documents the action taken on the task on ‘location list’ on the ‘CEA’ space

ups; seeks alternate PR/S contacts for updates and asks for an updated contact 
then follow up till the enrollment is completed.

▪

(INN or OON), and the SDD is notified, the provider is authorized to begin
seeing patients covered by the specific insurance plan.

1- Purpose: 
This procedure establishes a standardized framework for the creation and management of 
provider portals within the Credentialing Department. It ensures accurate setup, optimal 
functionality, facilitating the timely and precise submission of claims, authorizations, and 
other billing transaction purposes. Additionally, it supports credentialing processes, including 
the demo update for certain insurance providers.  
2- Scope: 
This procedure outlines the procedures for the creation and ongoing support of provider 
portals within the Credentialing Department. It encompasses the full lifecycle of these 
portals, from initial setup and registration to continuous maintenance, ensuring efficient 
functionality for billing and credentialing purposes   
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
3.2- Internal "Credentialing Manual" Sheet. 
3.3- Procedures: Data Collection and Verification 
3.4- Procedure: Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) Setup

4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- Abbreviations:   
4.1.1- B&A : Billing and Authorization 
4.1.2- B&AD: Billing and Authorization Department 
4.1.3- CS 
: Credentialing Specialist 
4.1.4- E&PS : EDIs and Portals Space 
4.1.5- EOBs : Explanation of Benefits 
4.1.6- OTP : One-Time Password 
4.1.7- PL 
: Portal List (code: PS/CRE/F-15) 
4.1.8- SCS 
: Senior Credentialing Specialist 
4.1.9- SDD : Service Delivery Department 
4.2- Terms and Definitions:   
4.2.1- Credentialing Manual: A detailed internal manual used by the Credentialing 
Department containing step-by-step instructions for portal creation, demographic 
updates, and EFT/ERA/EDI setup processes. 
4.2.2- EDIs and Portals Space (E&PS): A designated workspace on ClickUp used for 
managing electronic data interchange and portal-related tasks and communications. 
4.2.3- One-Time Password (OTP): A temporary verification code sent to a registered email 
or phone number for security authentication during portal setup or login. 
4.2.4- Portal Creation: The process of registering and setting up provider accounts on 
insurance or clearinghouse web portals to enable electronic transactions and 
information management. 
4.2.5- Portal List (PL): A master tracking list within the Portals Space on ClickUp that 
contains all portal creation requests and their status (code: PS/CRE/F-15). 
4.2.6- Registration Code: A unique identifier provided by insurance companies or 
clearinghouses that is required to complete the portal registration process. 
4.2.7- Tax ID Linking: The process of connecting a provider's tax identification number to 
their portal account, often required for billing functionality and account verification.

Title 
Responsibility 
CEO 
- Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this 
procedure achieve their intended outcomes. 
- Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are 
integrated within business processes.

Specialist 
- Responsible for overseeing portal creation process and 
resolving escalations 
Credentialing 
Specialist (CS) 
- Executes the portal creation process from request to 
completion 
- Collects and verifies required data for portal registration 
- Follows step-by-step instructions in the Credentialing Manual 
for portal creation 
- Updates ClickUp tasks and Login Sheets with portal 
credentials and status 
- Handles portal access issues and coordinates with technical 
support when needed 
B&AD/SDD 
- Responsible for submitting portal creation requests via 
ClickUp with complete required information 
Provider 
- Provides verification codes (OTP) when sent to their personal 
email or phone during portal setup.  
- Assists with password resets or account recovery when 
required.  
 
6- Procedures: 
6.1- Initiating Portal Creation  
6.1.1- If the portal is required for billing purposes, including authorization checks and claim 
submissions, once the provider’s enrollment with the insurance company is complete.  
• The Billing & Authorization Department (B&AD) or Service Delivery Department 
(SDD) submits a request to the CS to create a new portal for a healthcare provider.  
• The portal creation task is reflected in the Portals List (code: PS/CRE-F-16) in the 
Portals Space of the group in ClickUp.  
• The CS assigns a due date for the Task

6.1.2- If the portal is required for the following reasons: 
• EFT/ERA setup to enable electronic payments and electronic transfer of EOBs. 
• Demographic updates / account attestation to ensure accurate provider information.  
• The CS creates the portal once the provider’s enrollment is finalized.  
• The login sheet (code: PS/CRE-F-05) is updated with the account credentials and any 
additional information such as security questions and email linked to the account.  
6.2- Portal Creation Process  
6.2.1- Collect the data required for the portal Creation (Some portals need specific 
information like (e.g., claim or draft numbers from B&AD) 
• If any required data is missing:  
o The Credentialing Specialist (CS) asks for the information on the existing Portal 
Creation Request task on ClickUp.  
• If no tasks were created: 
o CS reaches out to the B&A assistant manager to provide the information   
6.2.2- Checking the Credentialing Manual: 
• CS reviews the Credentialing Manual in Portal creation Section for:  
o Insurance/Clearinghouse - specific portal links.  
o Step-by-step registration instructions. 
6.2.3- Obtain Portal Creation Instructions (If Unavailable): 
• If the Credentialing Manual is not familiar with the instructions:  
o CS contacts the insurance provider or Clearinghouse to obtain the portal link and 
registration steps.  
o CS proceeds to create the portal once all information is obtained.  
o Updates the Credentialing Manual (code: PS/CRE-F-17) with the new link and 
registration instructions for future reference.  
6.2.4- Submission of the Portal creation request:   
• The CS opens the portal link and clicks on Sign Up to initiate the registration process. 
• Fills in all required information (e.g., Group Name, Group NPI, Tax ID, Provider 
Name, Individual NPI)

o Registration Code for the portal creation (If Applicable):  
▪ Request Registration Code, if a registration code is required before or after the 
setting of the Password and User ID for the portal: 
▪ CS requests it by email or via call with the insurance or the clearinghouse portal 
technical support    
▪ Once received, the CS enters the code to complete the portal creation.  
▪ Follow-Up Actions in this case: 
• The CS follows up with the insurance providers or clearinghouses via email or 
phone to ensure the portal creation process moves forward.  
• If there is no confirmation or if delays occur, the CS contacts the Portal 
Support Team to verify the status of the portal setup and address any technical 
issues in the portal creation. 
6.3- Portal Creation Completion: 
6.3.1- Completion Status Update: 
• The CS updates the task status to "Completed" once the portal creation is finalized or 
the Tax ID linking is complete, depending on the portal’s requirements.  
• Once the portal setup is confirmed, the CS updates the Group Login Sheet (code: 
PS/CRE-F-05) with:  
o Portal credentials (username, password).  
o Portal link.  
o Portal name.  
o Login PIN (if applicable). 
o Any additional information such as, security questions and email linked to the 
account. 
6.4- Handing the Portal Access Issues (If arises):  
6.4.1-If there are issues with portal login credentials or if the portal is locked, the B&A 
Department submits a request to unlock the portal. 
The CS follows these steps to address the portal login issue:  
• CS will attempt to log in using the credentials provided, if access is denied or the portal 
being locked, the CS moves to the next steps.  
• Contacts Technical Support for assistance to either reset the password or resolve the 
underlying issue with the portal’s access.

1- Purpose: 
This procedure outlines the process for demographic updates for providers and new service 
locations of PT of the City into insurance networks (adding service location to PTOC or 
adding service location to a provider). It ensures PT of the City (PTOC) maintains accurate, 
compliant, and insurer-approved profiles to prevent claim denials, streamline patient access, 
and uphold operational efficiency. 
2- Scope: 
2.1- This procedure defines the process for managing all demographic updates for PT Of the 
City (PTOC), ensuring accurate provider and location information across insurance 
networks 
2.2- The procedure covers two primary scenarios: 
• New Provider Onboarding: Adding new rendering providers to existing PTOC in-
surance contracts. 
• Existing Information Maintenance: Adding new service locations or updating prac-
tice addresses for existing providers. 
2.3- This scope includes all stages from data verification through submission, follow-up, and 
final system updates (ClickUp, Credentialing Plan). 
2.4- It applies to all Credentialing staff and interdependent teams.

4.2- Terms and Definitions:  
4.2.1- CAQH (Council for Affordable Quality Healthcare): A platform where 
healthcare providers can enter and maintain their professional and practice infor-
mation, such as work experience, practice addresses, and education that helps en-
sure that all their credentials are up to date and health plans can access this infor-
mation for the credentialing process.  
4.2.2- ClickUp: A platform used by the Credentialing department to help manage tasks, 
projects, and workflows. 
4.2.3- Credentialing Manual: A detailed manual used by the Credentialing department 
for enrollment, demographic updates, EFT/ERA/EDI setup, and contact infor-
mation for each insurance 
4.2.4- Credentialing Plan: A Google sheet where all PTOC locations are added in sub-
sheets is used to specify which rendering provider is at which servicing location.

4.2.5- Demographic Update: A process of updating the provider and/or group infor-
mation with insurance companies, adding a new service location under the group, 
or adding a location to a provider under the group. 
4.2.6- eMedNY: The portal where healthcare providers can enroll in New York State's 
Medicaid program, update their information, and manage their enrollment status 
for Medicaid services. 
4.2.7- ePaces: The official website for New York State's Medicaid program, where pro-
viders can access billing information, submit claims, provider enroll-
ment/demographic update information, and manage patient services related to 
Medicaid. 
4.2.8- Google Drive File: A file created by the CS for each provider under PTOC is used 
by the CS to store the provider documents. 
4.2.9- Location list: A list on the PTOC space on ClickUp that has the tasks of a provid-
er/group to document and track updates and information of providers/group. 
4.2.10- Network Status: The status that indicates a provider is INN (In-Network) or OON 
(Out-of-Network). 
4.2.11- NPI (National Provider Identifier): A unique identifying number for any 
healthcare provider or group and organization used in the U.S. 
4.2.12- NPPES: A system where healthcare providers can apply for and manage their Na-
tional Provider Identifier (NPI), a unique number used for identifying them in 
healthcare transactions. 
4.2.13- PT Of The City Space: A space on ClickUp used by the Credentialing department 
that has all PTOC files, locations, and providers' data. 
4.2.14- Rendering provider: The provider that is providing the services. 
4.2.15- Registration License: A license provided by the state that permits the provider to 
practice in this state. 
4.2.16- Servicing location: Clinic’s location where the healthcare services are provided to 
the patients.

6- Procedures: 
6.1- The demographic update process could be either the addition of a new rendering 
provider to PTOC or a change to existing service location information. 
6.2- New provider joining PTOC: 
6.2.1- Announcement: 
• HR team provides hiring clearance via the Hiring Clearance List in ClickUp. 
• CS creates a task on the Location List within the ‘PT Of The City’ space on ClickUp 
(code: PS/CRE-F-07). 
6.2.2- The data is collected and verified, check the Data Collection and verification 
procedure. 
6.2.3- Prerequisite Setup: 
• CS completes prerequisite setups: 
o Add provider to Therabill (refer to Credentialing Manual Insurance Enrollment 
sub-sheet) 
o Complete CAQH setup (refer to Credentialing Manual Portal Creation sub-sheet  
o Submit Medicare/PECOS enrollment (refer to Credentialing Manual Medicare 
sub-sheet) 
o Complete Workers' Compensation setup (refer to Credentialing Manual Section 
B) 
6.2.4- Proceed with demographic updates: 
• CS proceeds with the demographic update based on the group’s network status 
(either INN or OON): 
o In-network update: 
▪ CS determines the submission method based on the steps on the Credentialing 
Manual. 
• If Website Submission: 
o CS checks credentialing manual for steps and link. 
o Fills the form on the website and reviews the inserted data and submits the form. 
o Documents the action taken on the location list on the PTOC space on ClickUp 
using a template. 
o Assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status to ‘Application Submitted’ 
and sets a due date.

o Writes the email using a template found in the credentialing manual email 
templates subsheet (code: PS/CRE-F-08) and attaches the filled form/s along with 
the required documents. 
o Sends the enrollment email with Subject: New Enrollment - [Provider Name]. 
o Documents the action taken on the location list on the PTOC space on ClickUp 
using a template. 
o Assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status to ‘Application Submitted’ 
and sets a due date. 
• If Fax Submission: 
o CS checks credentialing manual for steps, form/s and insurance fax number. 
o Fills the form and reviews the inserted data. 
o Sends the enrollment fax with Subject: New Enrollment - [Provider Name]. 
o Documents the action taken on the location list on the PTOC space on ClickUp 
using a template (code: PS/CRE-F-07). 
o Assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status to ‘Application Submitted’ 
and sets a due date. 
• If Mail Submission: 
o CS checks credentialing manual for steps, forms and ‘mail-to’ address. 
o Fills the form and reviews the inserted data. 
o Sends the forms to the provider via email using a template (code: PS/CRE-F-08). 
o Documents the action taken on the location list on the PTOC space on ClickUp 
using a template (code: PS/CRE-F-07). 
o Assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status to ‘Application Submitted’ 
and sets a due date. 
o Out-of-network update: 
• If Fax Submission: 
o CS checks credentialing manual for steps, form/s and insurance fax number. 
o Fills and signs the form/s and reviews the inserted data.

• If Through Portal:

o Documents the action taken on the location list on the PTOC space on ClickUp

6.2.5- Follow up: 
• The follow up method differs:  
o If Website Submission: 
▪ CS checks manual for the method of communication with the insurance. 
▪ Calls or emails provider relations or services and provides either the reference 
number or the date the application was submitted. 
▪ If there are updates, CS documents the action on the task and changes the task 
status and due date. 
▪ If no updates, CS documents the action and changes the due date. 
▪ CS escalates after 4 follow-ups; seeks alternate PR/S contacts. 
▪ Contacts alternate PR/S for updates. 
▪ Follows up until the new provider is added under the group. 
o If Email Submission: 
▪ CS sends a follow up email on the same request that was sent. 
▪ If there are updates, CS documents the action and changes the task status and 
due date.

▪ If no updates, CS documents the action and changes the due date. 
▪ CS escalates after 4 follow-ups; seeks alternate PR/S contacts. 
▪ Contacts alternate PR/S for updates. 
▪ Follows up until the new provider is added under the group. 
o If Fax Submission: 
▪ CS calls PR/S and provides the date of submission. 
▪ If there are updates, CS documents the action and changes the task status and 
due date. 
▪ If no updates, CS documents the action and changes the due date. 
▪ CS escalates after 4 follow-ups; seeks alternate PR/S contacts. 
▪ Contacts alternate PR/S for updates. 
▪ Follows up until the new provider is added under the group. 
o If Mail Submission: 
▪ CS calls PR/S and provides the date of submission. 
▪ If the form/s are received, CS documents the action, changes the task status 
and due date, and follows up regularly until the update is completed. 
▪ If the form/s are not received, CS checks USPS tracking if available, and if not 
received, asks the provider to mail the documents again. 
▪ If the forms are rejected, CS investigates the reason for rejection and asks the 
provider to mail the corrected documents. 
▪ Follows up until the new provider is added under the group. 
6.2.6- Update: 
• Click up updates: 
o CS writes a clear comment including the effective date, and how they obtained 
the information on the task on ClickUp. 
o Changes the status of the task to complete and removes the due date. 
• Credentialing Plan updates: 
o CS updates WebPT Billing sheet then credentialing plan (code: PS/CRE-F-09) 
sheet according to the service location corresponding to the new rendering 
provider.

1- Purpose: 
To define the standard process for issuing, receiving, and managing company devices, as well 
as controlling all IT-related onboarding and offboarding activities, including user accounts, 
system access, assigned devices, and email management, to ensure proper tracking, security, 
and documentation. 
2- Scope: 
This procedure covers the end-to-end IT support cycle, including resolving IT tickets, 
onboarding new employees, and offboarding exiting employees. It ensures timely issue 
resolution, proper preparation and provisioning of devices and accounts for new hires, as well 
as secure retrieval of assets, access revocation, and data handling for departing staff—all in 
alignment with IT security and operational standards. 
3- References: 
- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- Technical Support: 
Technical support is a service provided to help users solve technical problems they 
encounter when using devices, software, systems, or electronic services. The goal of 
technical support to ensure that these devices and programs work correctly.

4.2- Hardware: 
Hardware refers to all the tangible, physical parts of a computer on any electronic device. 
In order words, it encompasses everything that can be seen and touched, whether internal 
or external. 
4.3- Software: 
Software is a collection of programs commands, and instructions that control how 
computers and all electronic operate. In other words, it’s the intangible part of the device 
that directs the hardware to perform its tasks. 
 
5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation: 
Title 
Responsibility 
CEO 
• Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this 
procedure achieve their intended outcomes. 
• Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are 
integrated within business processes. 
All employee 
• Using assigned devices according to IT policies. 
• Maintaining device and account security. 
• Reporting technical issues promptly. 
• Returning all devices and access accounts during offboarding. 
• Using company emails and systems strictly for work purposes. 
All Managers / 
Supervisors 
• Ensuring employees follow IT policies and device usage rules. 
• Approving device requests and access permissions. 
IT Department 
• Preparing, configuring, and issuing devices to employees. 
• Troubleshooting and resolving technical issues. 
• Maintaining device records and updating inventory. 
• Ensuring proper setup of accounts, emails, and system access. 
• Collecting and checking devices during offboarding. 
 
6- Procedures: 
6.1- IT Onboarding: 
6.1.1-Once the hiring process is completed and the new hire’s start date is confirmed, HR 
sends an email to the IT Department informing them of the new employee and 
requesting the preparation of the assigned laptop. 
6.1.2- Upon receiving HR’s email notification, the IT Department prepares the laptop, 
completes the required details in receipt acknowledgment form (code: PS/IT-F-01), 
and records the asset information before handing it over to the new hire.

6.1.3- The employee reviews and signs the handover form, confirming receipt of the 
laptop and understanding of usage policy. The IT department then records laptop 
specifications (model, serial number, date of issue, etc.) and retains the signed form 
for as long as the employee works in the company, and one year after. 
6.1.4- The IT Department is responsible for creating, managing, and deactivating all 
company-related user accounts on internal and external platforms, including but not 
limited to WEBPT, The Voice, Info Email, Intermedia, and any other authorized 
systems. 
 
6.2- IT Support: 
6.2.1- The employee reports the laptop issue to their direct manager immediately after the 
problem occurs. 
6.2.2- Upon manager approval, the employee proceeds to the IT department submits a 
support ticket via Email or via Voice describing the problem in support request form 
code (PS/OpEx-F-01) (type of issue, when it occurred, any relevant notes).  
6.2.3- The IT technician performs an initial assessment of the device to identify the cause 
and type of issue (software or hardware related). 
6.2.4- After evaluation, the technician informs the employee of the estimated time required 
for repair on the ticket. 
6.2.5- Minor issues are repaired internally by the IT technician within the estimated time. 
If issues include device damage or require hardware replacement, the IT technician 
coordinates with management to investigate the possibility of repair or replacement. 
6.2.6- Once the issue is resolved or an action plan is determined, the IT technician 
completes the fault report form, (code: PS/IT-F-02), which includes: 
- Employee name and device information 
- Description of the issue 
- Actions taken 
- Date and time of resolution 
- Technician’s signature 
- This report is retained for at least three years. 
6.2.7- Once the issue is resolved, the IT technician closes the ticket and retaining it for at 
least 3 years. 
6.2.8- The IT technician produces a monthly report (code: PS/IT-F-04) summarizing 
incidents and repair times for performance analysis.

6.3- IT Offboarding: 
6.3.1- The IT Department receives an official resignation notification from HR via email. 
6.3.2- IT reviews the Employee COB Data Center Sheet (code: PS/IT-F-03) and the 
Onboarding Form (code: PS/IT-F-01) to verify all devices, accounts, and assets 
assigned to the employee. 
6.3.3- The employee proceeds to the IT department to return all company property, 
including his laptop, charger, mouse, keyboard, and other peripherals. 
6.3.4- The IT inspects the returned items and determines if there are any issues or damages 
and records his findings on the asset return form code (PS/HR-F03) indicating the 
type of damage (if any) and notify the HR department if further action is needed via 
email.

1- Purpose: 
This procedure ensures effective control of company inventory, supplies, and software 
licenses required to deliver services in compliance with ISO 9001:2015. 
2- Scope: 
This procedure applies to the receipt, storage, dispatch, control, and disposal of equipment, 
technical supplies, and software licenses used to support the company’s services. It applies to 
the use or management of company assets. 
3- References: 
The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- Software License:  
A legal agreement between the software creator and the end user, granting rights to use 
and distribute the software under specific terms. It outlines user responsibilities, limits 
claims against the owner, and may restrict how the software can legally be used. 
4.2- Item Tag:  
A unique identification code assigned to each controlled item. 
4.3- Quarantine Area:  
A designated area for devices that are unfit for use or under inspection.

5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation: 
Title 
Responsibility 
CEO 
Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this 
procedure achieve their intended outcomes. 
Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are 
integrated within business processes. 
IT Manager 
Approves specifications, allocation, and disposal. 
IT Storekeeper 
Approves specifications, allocation, and disposal. 
Purchasing  
Supplier coordination and purchasing. 
Quality Management Compliance monitoring. 
 
6- Procedures: 
6.1- Receiving Items: 
6.1.1- IT specialist receives items against approved purchase orders. 
6.1.2- IT specialist verifies quantity, specifications, serial numbers, warrantee documents, 
and item condition before signing off the receipt. IT specialist refuses receipt if 
item quality or quantity do not match the purchase order and informs procurement.  
6.1.3- Record the receiving item in receiving voucher form (code: PS/IT-F-05). 
6.1.4- Record the receiving item in IT receiving register (code: PS/IT-F-06), and the item 
register (code: PS/IT-F-07). 
6.1.5- In case of item return, the IT specialist records the reason of return and any addition 
details in return form (code: PS/IT-F-09). 
6.2- Storage: 
6.2.1- Equipment is stored in secured, access-controlled areas. 
6.2.2- Items are classified using item tags, item tags serve to uniquely identify items 
throughout their life cycle within the company.  
6.3- Software License Control: 
6.3.1- IT specialists maintain the software license register, monitor validity and usage. IT 
specialists never allow unlicensed or unapproved software installation.
