6.4- Issuing & Allocation: 
6.4.1- Items are issued (dispatched) against approved request via email. 
6.4.2- Configure devices with approved software. 
6.4.3- Record allocation and user acknowledgment. 
6.4.4- Record the issued item in issue voucher form (code: PS/IT-F-08). The form shall be 
signed by the recipient and the IT specialist. 
6.4.5- In case of item return, the IT specialist records the reason of return and ant addition 
details in return form (code: PS/IT-F-09) 
6.5- Inventory & Audit: 
6.5.1- Conduct periodic inventory checks monthly and quarterly. All inventory checks 
shall be documented using the inventory check form (code: PS/IT-F-10). 
6.5.2- Reconcile physical assets with records. 
6.5.3- Report discrepancies and take corrective actions. 
6.5.4- Monitor stock shortages and request items approaching depletion. 
6.6- Disposal of IT Assets: 
6.6.1- Isolate obsolete or damaged equipment in quarantine area. 
6.6.2- Perform secure data wiping. 
6.6.3- Dispose through approved methods with documentation in disposal committee form 
(code: PS/IT-F-11) yearly.

1- Purpose: 
- To define the purchasing procedures and the criteria for evaluating service providers, as 
well as conducting periodic evaluations to ensure fulfillment of supply commitments. 
- To define and describe the purchasing process to verify that purchased materials conform to 
the required specifications. 
2- Scope: 
This procedure applies to all purchasing activities and supplier evaluations related to 
products, services, software, and IT-related supplies. 
3- References: 
- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- Requirements: 
A general term referring to all materials and services required by the company for 
production and operational activities. 
4.2- Materials: 
A general term referring to all tangible items required by the company, such as supplies, 
spare parts, and similar items.

4.3- Services: 
A general term referring to all intangible items required to ensure the continuity of the 
company’s operations and production, such as maintenance, training, and consultancy 
services. 
4.4- Requirements Planning: 
The process of identifying requirements and determining the methods and means of 
procuring them in accordance with a defined time plan. 
4.5- Purchase Requisition: 
A form prepared by the requesting department to specify the quantity, specifications of 
the item to be purchased, the required delivery dates, and the delivery location. 
4.6- Request for Quotation (RFQ): 
A form prepared by the Head of the Local Purchasing Department to obtain price 
quotations from different suppliers for the same item. 
4.7- Purchase Order: 
A form prepared by the Purchasing Manager to execute the purchasing process with the 
selected supplier. 
5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation: 
Title 
Responsibility 
CEO 
• Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this 
procedure achieve their intended outcomes. 
• Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements 
are integrated within business processes. 
IT Manager 
• Approves specifications, allocation, and disposal. 
6- Procedures: 
6.1- Identification of Purchasing Needs: 
6.1.1- The requesting department shall inform the information technology department of 
its purchasing requirements.

6.1.2- A purchase requisition form (code: PS/IT-F-12) shall be prepared and submitted to 
the purchasing officer. The form shall include the following details: 
- All relevant purchasing data 
- Item name, reason for request, unit of measure, required quantity, and current 
stock balance 
- Required delivery period 
- Requesting department and justification 
- Required quantity and delivery timeline 
6.1.3- The Purchase Requisition shall be approved by the requesting department either by 
signature or via official email. 
6.2- Purchase Requisition Approval Process: 
6.2.1- The purchasing officer shall collect all purchase requisitions and submit them to the 
company executive manager for approval. 
6.3- Purchasing Execution Process: 
6.3.1- Upon approval of the purchase requisition, the head of the IT department shall 
perform the following: 
- Select the appropriate supplier from the approved supplier list (code: PS/IT-F-13). 
- Issue a purchase order (code: PS/IT-F-14), approved by the finance manager, 
including the following information: 
• Item name and item code as stated in the Purchase Requisition 
• Quantity to be supplied and delivery schedule (where applicable) 
• Purchase Requisition number and supplier quotation reference (if available) 
• Delivery location (if applicable) 
6.3.2- If the item is available from more than one approved supplier, price quotations shall 
be obtained using the request for quotation (RFQ) Form (code: PS/IT-F-15), and a 
comparison shall be conducted to select the most competitive offer. 
6.3.3- If the required item is not available from approved suppliers list (code: PS/IT-F-13), 
the head of the local purchasing department shall request quotations from non-
approved suppliers based on the value of the purchase.

to completing the purchase and document the evaluation in evaluation suppliers 
form (code: PS/IT-F-19). 
6.3.5- Any special terms and conditions related to the purchase order (code: PS/IT-F-14) 
shall be printed on the reverse side and shall be considered an integral part of the 
purchase order. 
6.3.6- The purchasing officer shall distribute copies of the approved purchase order as 
follows: 
- Original copy to the supplier for execution 
- One copy retained by the purchasing department (electronic or hard copy) 
6.3.7- The purchasing officer shall follow up on purchase requisitions (code: PS/IT-F-12) 
and purchase orders (code: PS/IT-F-14) with suppliers until delivery and inspection 
are completed, based on the purchase follow-up register (code: PS/IT-F-16). 
6.3.8- Upon delivery of purchased items, the storekeeper shall acknowledge receipt by 
signing the supplier’s delivery note or a copy of the purchase invoice, indicating 
that the items are received subject to inspection. 
6.4- Price Comparison and Supplier Selection Process: 
6.4.1- The purchasing officer shall compare supplier quotations received in response to 
RFQs (code: PS/IT-F-17) when the same item with identical specifications and 
quality level is available from more than one approved supplier. 
6.4.2- The purchasing officer shall ensure that all quotations are complete, clear, and 
properly documented. 
6.4.3- After evaluating the quotations, the purchasing officer shall select the best offer and 
issue the purchase order (code: PS/IT-F-14) to the selected supplier. 
6.5- Procurement of Services: 
6.5.1- The purchasing officer shall be responsible for contacting and dealing with all 
service providers, including but not limited to: 
- Maintenance service providers. 
- Training and consultancy companies. 
- Any other service providers required by the company.

1- Purpose: 
The objective of this procedure is to establish a systematic and standardized process for 
auditing medical notes to ensure accuracy, completeness, and compliance with organizational, 
regulatory, and quality standards. This procedure provides guidance for reviewing all patient 
encounters, ensuring that every appointment has a corresponding documented note in the 
system. It also outlines the management of complex, incomplete, or denied notes to guarantee 
proper resolution and maintain documentation integrity. The organization aims to enhance the 
reliability of medical records, support continuity of patient care, reduce the risk of errors or 
omissions, and ensure that all documentation meets clinical, legal, and operational 
requirements. Additionally, the process promotes accountability among healthcare providers, 
facilitates transparent reporting, and supports continuous quality improvement initiatives 
within the medical auditing framework. 
2- Scope: 
2.1- Applies to the Medical Auditing Team and all staff involved in medical record review and 
documentation.  
2.2- Covers all medical notes done by clients’ medical staff to identify, confirm, and resolve 
missing, inconsistent, or incomplete medical notes.

3- References: 
3.1- The Internation Standard ISO 9001:2015 – Quality Management Systems 
3.2- HIPAA - (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act)  
3.3- CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) Guidelines – Documentation, 
billing, and compliance requirements. 
4- Terms / Terminology: 
4.1- EMR system: 
It is an organized collection of patient’s information that is stored electronically and can 
be accessed and updated by authorized healthcare providers. 
4.2- Medical Audit: 
A systematic review of patient records, billing documentation, and coding accuracy to 
ensure compliance and accuracy. 
4.3- Patient Medical Record:  
A version of a patient's medical history and healthcare data, stored in an EHR system. It 
includes all the necessary information to support the clinical decisions and care of the 
patient over time, may be stored in an Electronic Health Record (EHR) system or in paper 
format. 
4.4- Medical Auditor:  
A trained professional responsible for conducting medical audits, ensuring accuracy, and 
providing feedback for compliance and quality improvement. 
4.5- Medical Auditing Supervisor:  
The Medical Auditing Supervisor is responsible for overseeing and coordinating the daily 
activities of the medical auditing team. This role ensures that audit processes are 
performed accurately, efficiently, and in compliance with organizational and regulatory 
standards 
4.6- Medical Auditor Team Leader:  
The Medical Auditing Team Leader oversees a team of auditors, ensuring accurate and 
timely completion of audits. They assign tasks, monitor performance, resolve complex 
cases, manage incoming requests and communications from billing and authorization 
teams, and support the onboarding of new providers. The role focuses on maintaining audit 
quality, improving processes, and providing guidance and training to the auditing team.

4.7- Medical Auditing Manager:  
The Medical Auditing Team Manager leads and oversees the auditing team, ensuring 
accurate, efficient, and compliant audit processes. The manager assigns and prioritizes 
tasks, monitors performance, resolves complex cases, coordinates with other departments, 
and communicates with senior management and healthcare providers regarding urgent 
issues. They are responsible for maintaining audit quality, improving processes, and 
supporting team development. 
4.8- Front desk: 
Helps to confirm patient appointments and removes no-show appointments when 
instructed. 
4.9- System Credentials:  
A username and password assigned to the auditor or healthcare provider, enabling them to 
log in to the system and access patient charts, schedules, and relevant medical 
documentation. 
4.10- Click Up: 
Click Up is a cloud-based project management and productivity platform used to plan, 
track, and manage tasks, projects, and team workflows. It allows teams to organize work, 
assign tasks, monitor progress, set priorities, and ensure deadlines are met efficiently. 
4.11- SOAP Notes:  
A method of documenting patient information using four categories: Subjective, 
Objective, Assessment, and Plan. It is a widely used format for medical documentation. 
- Subjective: The patient's reported symptoms, history, and experiences. 
- Objective: Measurable data such as physical exams, test results, and observations. 
- Assessment: The healthcare provider’s analysis of the situation, including diagnoses. 
- Plan: The treatment or actions to be taken, including therapy goals and interventions. 
4.12- Dx: Diagnosis:  
The identification of a medical condition based on symptoms, signs, and tests. 
4.13- ICD:  
International Classification of Diseases, a coding system for diagnoses and conditions. 
4.14- CPT:  
Current Procedural Terminology – A set of codes used to describe medical and diagnostic 
services.

4.15- LTG: 
Long-Term Goals – Refers to goals, treatments, or outcomes anticipated to take a longer 
time frame. 
4.16- STG:  
Short-Term Goals– Refers to goals or outcomes expected to be achieved in a shorter time 
frame. 
4.17- Initial Examination Note “IE”:  
The first comprehensive record created after evaluating a patient, detailing their medical 
history, presenting condition, assessment findings, and initial treatment plan. 
4.18- Daily Note “DN”: 
A brief entry made during each patient follow up visit, summarizing the treatment 
provided, the patient’s status, and any immediate changes in care plans. 
4.19- Progress Note “PN”: 
A record documenting a patient's treatment, responses, and any updates during ongoing 
care. It tracks changes in the patient’s condition and provides continuity for subsequent 
care “Re-evaluating”. 
4.20- Discharge Summary “D/C”: 
A detailed report provided at the conclusion of a patient’s care. It includes a summary of 
their condition, treatments provided, outcomes achieved, and recommendations for further 
care or monitoring. 
4.21- Discharge Note: 
A concise document written at the end of a patient’s care, summarizing the reason for the 
discharge and any instructions or recommendations for follow-up care. 
4.22- Patients Schedule:  
feature within the system where appointments are organized and managed for individual 
patients. 
4.23- Patient's Appointments “appt”: 
Scheduled dates and times for a patient to receive evaluations, treatments, or follow-ups, 
ensuring timely and organized care delivery. 
4.24- Authorized Visits:  
The number of treatment sessions approved by an insurer or payer for a patient, based on 
their diagnosis and medical necessity.

6- Procedures: 
6.1- Medical Note Verification: 
6.1.1- The medical auditing manager assigns auditors to each client or client’s clinic using 
auditing distribution form code (PS/MA-F-01). 
6.1.2- The assigned medical auditor begins work after clinic closure, once all patient 
documentation is completed by the provider. 
6.1.3- Note-Schedule Comparison: compares opened medical notes with the daily schedule 
to ensure all attending patients have documented notes. 
6.1.4- Missing Notes Handling: If discrepancies are identified between scheduled 
appointments and completed medical notes, document the missing notes in the medical 
tracking sheet and notify the provider to complete them. 
6.1.5- Consultation Notes: If there is any patient consultation, submit it via the designated 
Google Form code (PS/MA-F-02). 
6.1.6- The audit is performed in accordance with the criteria outlined in Documentation Tips 
Appendix, code (PS/MA-A-06). 
6.2- Audit of Completed Notes: 
6.2.1- Check accuracy of the following fields for each note: 
1. Patient Age/Gender 
2. Date of the note 
3. ICD-10 and Diagnosis 
4. Patient History 
5. Chief Complaint 
6. Objectives 
7. Assessment 
8. Problem List & Goals 
9. Plan of Care 
10. Billing 
6.2.2- The medical auditor checks the internal consistency of all medical notes, and the 
consistency of the medical note with the patient’s referral. 
6.2.3- Note Finalization: Finalize the completed medical notes under the correct healthcare 
provider, according to the credentials sheet.

6.2.4- Complex Notes Escalation: If a medical note is too complex, escalate to the Senior 
Medical Auditor, who coordinates with departments and the provider for resolution, 
with follow-up by the Team Leader and oversight by the Manager. Communication 
via chat / emails with provider and billing department. 
6.2.5- The audit is performed in accordance with the guidelines outlined in Internal Medical 
Notes Guidelines code (PS/MA-A-02), as well as the guidelines in Documentation 
Guidelines code (PS/MA-A-03), and the guidelines in Documentation Tips code 
(PS/MA-A-04). 
6.3- Double Review and Finalization Process: 
6.3.1- Review of Auditor Work: 
The Supervisor shall access the audit dashboard to identify audit cases completed by 
the assigned auditor. Each medical note sheet must be carefully reviewed for 
completeness, accuracy, and adherence to established documentation standards. The 
Supervisor shall also verify that the auditor’s comments, justifications, and findings 
are clearly stated, logically supported, and in full compliance with the organization’s 
audit guidelines and procedures. 
6.3.2- Verification and Communication: 
             During this stage, the supervisor identifies any pending or incomplete medical notes 
that require further action. The supervisor communicates with the responsible 
physicians through approved channels such as email or the internal chat platform 
“Intermedia” to request necessary updates, clarifications, or corrections. All 
communications should be tracked and documented to ensure timely follow-up, 
response, and closure of outstanding cases in line with departmental timelines. 
6.3.3- Closure of Audit Notes: 
             Once the physicians complete the pending notes, the Supervisor verifies that all 
updates conform to documentation standards, compliance criteria, and quality 
requirements. After validation, the Supervisor proceeds to close the audit case in the 
dashboard system, ensuring that the information is accurate and complete. The closure 
date is recorded, and the audit status is updated to reflect the final outcome, ensuring 
traceability and accountability within the audit system.

6.4 Reporting: 
6.4.1- After all reviews and closures are completed, the Supervisor compiles a 
comprehensive summary report that includes details of reviewed and closed audit 
cases, as well as any identified discrepancies, delays, or recurring issues. This report 
provides a clear overview of audit outcomes and performance trends. The Supervisor 
then submits the finalized report to the Team Leader and Manager for review, 
discussion, and determination of any necessary corrective actions.  
6.4.2- The summary reports are composed by filtering the medical issues tracking sheet code 
(PS/MA-F-03) for still-missing or inaccurate notes.

Internal Medical Notes Guidelines  
1- If the patient is seen under: 
1.1- Direct Access (No Referral) – New York State Law: 
● You must perform a reexamination and obtain a referral after: 
○ 10 visits OR 
○ 30 days, whichever comes first. 
This is a legal requirement in NY, separate from Medicare or insurance rules. 
 
2- Progress Note: 
2.1- Purpose: 
● To document the patient's response to treatment 
● To reflect skilled care and medical necessity 
● Often used to support ongoing treatment and insurance reimbursement 
2.2- Timing: 
● Required at least every: 
○ 10 treatment visits 
○ or every 30 days, whichever comes first (matching Direct Access limits) 
○ Can be done more frequently depending on the payer or patient’s condition 
 
3- Reexamination Note: 
3.1- Purpose: 
● To formally reassess the patient after a defined treatment period 
● Required when: 
○ There is a significant change in condition 
○ There’s a discharge or need to renew the Plan of Care (POC) 
3.2- When to do a formal progress or re-exam note: 
● Progress note: Every 10 visits or 30 days is standard (depending on payer), but if you're 
only seeing them monthly, you should consider every visit a "progress" visit in your 
documentation. Just make it a more detailed Daily note.  
● If the patient comes infrequently (e.g., once/month), the 90-day rule usually triggers first. 
● Reexamination: Required if there is:

○ A significant change in status 
○ The patient is discharged and later returns. 
○ POC has expired and a new one must be created. 
○ Insurance requires a new evaluation (e.g., after surgery). 
 
4- Plan of Care Certification/Re-Certification: 
● The initial Plan of Care must be certified by a physician/NPP within 30 days of the first 
treatment. 
● If therapy continues beyond 90 days, a recertification is required (even if the patient hasn’t 
had 10 visits yet). 
 
5- For Commercial Insurance (non-Medicare): 
5.1- Follow the insurer's specific authorization rules, NOT Medicare rules unless: 
● The insurance requires progress reports every X visits or days. 
● The patient is being seen under a Medicare Advantage Plan (which may still follow 
Medicare-like documentation requirements — check the plan). 
 
6- Discharge Note - When Should It Be Done? 
6.1- A discharge note is required when: 
● The goals for the current plan of care (POC) have been met. 
● The patient is no longer benefiting from skilled PT (plateaued or non-compliant). 
● The patient has stopped attending (e.g., missed several visits without notice and you're 
closing the case). 
● The patient is being referred out (e.g., to an ortho or surgeon). 
● The patient completes care for a specific body region or episode.

Documentation Guidelines for Progress, Recertification, and Discharge Notes 
1- Progress Notes 
• Progress notes should be completed on the last authorized session or every 10 sessions, based 
on the number of visits since the last documented progress note  
• Re-examination notes should be used only when there is a significant change in the patient’s 
condition  
2- Recertification Notes (Medicare Patients) 
• Recertification notes are required every 90 days from the last one 
• For Medicare patients under Direct Access PT (without a physician referral), a certification 
note is required after 10 visits or within the first month, whichever comes first. 
• If the patient has not attended for a month or more, a progress note is not required due to the 
gap in attendance. A detailed daily note is sufficient unless: 
o The patient's authorization has expired 
o The Medicare Plan of Care (POC) has expired 
o A recertification is required 
3- Progress Notes for Extended Authorizations 
• When insurance approves 16, 20, or more sessions, progress notes should be completed every 
10 sessions, and again on the last visit of the authorized period. 
4- Discharge Notes 
4.1- A discharge note should be completed in any of the following situations: 
• 
The patient will begin treatment for a new body part (the current case should be 
discharged to allocate visits to the new case). 
• 
The patient chooses to end treatment voluntarily. 
• 
The patient has no remaining authorized visits, and authorization will not be renewed so 
they request last visit of an current authorization as a discharge note  
5- New Patients (Especially Medicare) 
• 
For all new patients especially Medicare patients verify that the body part documented in the 
note matches the diagnosis (ICD-10) stated in the referral found in eDocs within the patient's 
chart. 
• 
This ensures compliance and supports accurate documentation for billing and medical 
necessity.

Documentation Tips 
1- Common Mistakes to Avoid 
1.1 Documentation Errors 
• 
Failing to provide complete and detailed notes (missing exercises, dosage, patient 
response). 
• 
Inadequate progress reports (missing objective changes or justification for continued care). 
• 
Not updating the POC or recertifying within 90 days (Medicare rule). 
• 
Overuse of EMR templates without customization to the patient. 
• 
Forgetting to document skilled need and medical necessity. 
• 
Skipping reassessments intervals (e.g., every 10 visits). 
 
2- Billing and Coding Errors 
2.1- Upcoding without appropriate documentation to support higher complexity. 
2.2- Failing to use CQ (PTA) or CO (OTA) modifiers when applicable. 
 
3- Clinical Practice Issues 
3.1- Ignoring or not addressing patient-specific goals. 
3.2- Not documenting or assigning a Home Exercise Program (HEP). 
3.3- Providing non-functional or outdated interventions. 
3.4- Not Following Post-Operative Rehab Protocols. 
3.5- Update and progress your exercises load, Freq and intensity. 
3.6- Lack of outcome measure usage or failure to track progress. 
 
4- Ethical and Legal Mistakes 
4.1- Practicing outside the licensed scope (e.g., dry needling in unlicensed states). 
4.2- Not reporting patient injuries, falls, or adverse events during treatment. 
4.3- Providing unnecessary or non-skilled services just to extend visits. 
4.4- Failing to adhere to HIPAA or confidentiality regulations.

5- when it is Disability in Documentation Tips  
5.1- Use specific, measurable language to describe patient progress and outcomes. 
5-.2- Provide a clear assessment of the patient's condition and progress. 
5.3- Outline a detailed treatment plan, including any modifications. 
5.4- Regularly update and progress your exercises load, Freq and intensity. 
• 
Intensity & Resistance (Colored bands, Weights, Reps and Sets). 
5.5- Use standardized assessment, Questionnaires and outcome measures tools to track progress 
consistently. 
• 
Do not forget that some questionnaires are better with high scores and some are worse. 
o Rule:  
- Dex, Better outcomes with lower scores like ODI, NDI. 
- Functional named Index, Better outcomes with Higher scores like LEFS. 
o Match the pain level & MMT with the objective measures/questionnaires. 
o Link treatment goals to specific functional improvements in daily activities. 
o Include the patient's response to treatment and any changes in their condition. 
o Avoid vague phrases like “patient tolerated treatment well”. 
o Ensure each note shows medical necessity and skilled intervention. 
o Ensure documentation reflects the clinical reasoning behind treatment decisions. 
o Regularly update treatment goals based on patient progress.

4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- Click-Up:  
Cloud-based project management and productivity platform designed to help teams and 
individuals manage tasks, workflows, and collaboration in one place. 
4.2- EMR (Electronic Medical Records): 
It’s a digital version of a patient’s medical chart that is maintained within a single 
healthcare organization (e.g., a clinic or hospital). 
4.3- EHR (Electronic Health Records): 
It’s a broader digital record that can be shared across multiple healthcare organizations to 
provide a complete patient history. 
4.4- Medical Billing System:  
It’s a software solution designed to manage the billing, claims submission, and revenue 
cycle process for healthcare providers. It ensures accurate invoicing, payment tracking, 
and compliance with insurance requirements. 
4.5- Google Chat Space:  
It’s a collaborative messaging space within Google Chat where teams can communicate, 
share files, and manage tasks in an organized way. It is designed for ongoing 
conversations, project discussions, and team collaboration. 
4.6- Phone & Fax System:  
It’s a communication setup used in businesses and healthcare settings to handle voice calls 
and document transmissions securely and efficiently. 
4.7- Billing Sheet:  
It’s a structured document or digital report used in medical billing to track patient services, 
claims, and payments. It serves as a summary of billed charges, insurance details, and 
payment statuses, ensuring accurate revenue management. 
4.8- Scheduling System:  
It’s a software solution designed to automate and optimize the process of booking, 
managing, and tracking patient appointments in healthcare facilities. It helps streamline 
operations, reduce no-shows, and enhance patient satisfaction.

It’s a protected digital form used by a medical billing company to securely collect, store, 
and manage information about healthcare provider groups or facilities. This form ensures 
that sensitive data is handled securely while adhering to privacy regulations. 
4.10- Group Individual Form:  
It’s a protected digital form used by a medical billing company to securely collect, store, 
and manage information about individual healthcare providers (e.g., doctors, therapists, or 
clinicians). It ensures that sensitive personal and professional details are handled securely 
and in compliance with privacy regulations.

- Enrollment preferences (if not previously submitted) 
- Existing portal credentials (if applicable) 
- Approval letters or enrollment statuses for prior submissions 
6.1.7- Internal Service Onboarding Email: The Account Manager sends an onboarding 
email to the Credentialing Management Team (The Credentialing Manager and 
Senior), including: 
- Client name 
- Client’s title (Owner, Main PT, or both) 
- Group name 
- Specialty 
- Place of service (office, home, or both) 
- Confirmation of completed secured forms 
- Notification that all client details are available in the onboarding ClickUp task.

1- Purpose: 
      1.1- Identify denied claims and unpaid claims beyond 45 days.  
      1.2- Determine the causes and categorize each issue type 
      1.3- Take corrective actions to resolve identified issues promptly. 
      1.4- Streamline the follow-up process to enhance collections. 
2- Scope: 
2.1- This procedure applies to all billing team members responsible for claims management, 
follow-up, and resolution. 
2.2- It encompasses all claim types including commercial, workers' compensation, and no-
fault insurance. 
2.3- The process starts from identifying claims for collection and ends with closing resolved 
cases in the system. 
3- References: 
The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
4- Terms / Terminology: 
4.1- Auth (Valid): 
The claim was denied due to the absence of a valid authorization. 
4.2- Auth (Not Valid): 
The claim was denied despite having an existing authorization. 
4.3- Unspecified Denial: 
The denial lacks a clear reason. In this case, we must contact the insurance company for 
further clarification. 
4.4- Not invoiced because of hold: 
The invoice was not submitted due to the patient being placed on hold.

4.5- Not invoiced missing Referral: 
The invoice was not submitted because the required referral was not provided. 
4.6- Needs a COB update: 
The claim requires an update to the Coordination of Benefits (COB) information. 
4.7- Medical necessity denial: 
The claim was denied because the service was deemed not medically necessary by the 
insurance payer. 
4.8- Medical information request: 
The insurance payer has requested additional medical records or information to process 
the claim. 
4.9- Invalid diagnosis: 
Multiple diagnoses reported together that should not be combined. 
4.10- Timely filing limit: 
The claim was denied because it was submitted after the insurance company's filing 
deadline. 
4.11- Benefit maximum for this period has been reached: 
This denial, typically issued by the medical insurance company, occurs when the patient 
has reached the benefit maximum for the specified period. 
4.12- Negative IME (Independent Medical Examination): 
This typically applies to Workers' Compensation (WC) or No-Fault (NF) claims. A 
negative IME indicates that the patient has reached their Maximum Medical 
Improvement (MMI), meaning no further physical therapy is needed. 
      4.13- Provider demographics are not valid: 
The claim was denied because the provider’s demographic as the name or the NPI is 
incorrect or invalid, preventing proper claim processing. 
      4.14- Services not provided by primary care providers: 
The claim was denied because the services were not provided by the patient’s primary 
care provider, as required by the insurance policy. 
4.15- This service is not covered under the patient’s current plan: 
The claim was denied because the service is not included in the patient's current 
insurance plan benefits. 
4.16- Invalid diagnosis: 
The claim was denied because the patient’s insurance plan does not provide Out-of-
Network (OON) benefits for the services rendered.

5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation: 
Title 
Responsibility 
CEO 
• Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this 
procedure achieve their intended outcomes. 
• Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are 
integrated within business processes. 
Collection Agents 
• Execute claim filtration 
• Take Corrective Action Based on the Issue. 
• Record Actions Taken 
• Monitor and Track Progress 
Collection Seniors 
• Supervise filtration accuracy 
• Monitor follow-up 
• Prevent Recurring Issues 
 
6- Procedures: 
6.1- Filtration Process – Identifying Claims for Collection 
6.1.1- At the beginning of each month, the collection senior initiates the filtration process 
using the eligibility sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-01). This step is triggered by the 
monthly review cycle and the need to identify unpaid or denied claims. The agent 
filters out paid or adjusted claims and highlights those pending beyond 45 days, 
denied, or missing data. The filtered list is transferred to the Collection Sheet (code: 
PS/B&A-F-02) for corrective action. 
6.2- Take Corrective Action Based on the Issue. 
6.2.1- After claims are identified, the collection agent reviews each case to determine the 
reason for non-payment. Based on the issue, appropriate action is taken such as 
resubmitting a corrected claim, filing a dispute, or updating COB information. Each 
action is documented in the collection sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-02), including 
reference numbers or confirmation emails. Once actions are complete, updates are 
communicated to the collection senior and billing lead for tracking and verification. 
6.3- Monitor and Track Progress.  
6.3.1- Following corrective actions, collection agents monitor the progress of each claim 
through medical portals and payer updates in COB Account Data Sheet (code: 
PS/B&A-F-03). They update claim statuses (Pending, Resolved, Denied) in the 
collection sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-02) after every follow-up. If no update is 
received within the expected response time, follow-up calls, or portal checks are 
made.

changes procedures.

1- Purpose: 
This document describes the processes involving handling patient requests outsourced to the 
company, including fulfillment of these requests to ensure a consistent service level, 
minimization of potential errors, and the upholding of our commitment to the deliver the best 
service level to improve patient experience and operational efficiency 
2- Scope:

appointments, appointment cancellations and rescheduling, handling cancelled and no-show 
appointments, reconfirming appointments, coordinating transportation scheduling, managing 
patient complaints, and conducting customer satisfaction surveys.

4.2- Data Entry team:  
• Responsible for filling in the eligibility sheets for the cash flow process for both PT of 
the City / External Clients. 
• New patients Sheet update. 
4.3- Front Line:  
Agents responsible for taking the Incoming calls from patients handling all the inquiries, 
requested transactions and complaints. 
4.4- Back Office Team: 
Task oriented team and outbound calls team for confirmation, No upcoming, Hold, 
NON-AV & Insurance Approvals. 
4.5- Patient Pulse Unit: 
Responsible for the voice of the patient “Handling escalated Calls – Website messages – 
1 Star Google reviews – Voice tickets – patient’s complaints” and work toward patients’ 
retention. 
4.6- Account Managers: 
Responsible for the daily follow up on the Clinics daily tasks and to ensure that the daily 
checklists are fulfilled correctly. 
4.7- Virtual Front Desk Receptionist:  
Virtual replacement for the PCCs at the clinics handling the clinical patient’s transactions 
4.8- VFD: 
Is a digital solution, typically a software platform or remote service, that performs the 
functions of a traditional physical reception area without requiring dedicated on-site staff. 
It serves as the first point of contact for patients providing accessibility, information, and 
patients handling through the technology. 
4.9- Shadowing: 
Side by side monitoring with tenured agents. 
4.10- No Upcoming: 
Patients who did not schedule their next visit.

4.11- Zocdoc:  
It is an online platform and app that helps patients find and book medical appointments. 
It also allows patients to compare and rate doctors. 
4-12- Live Chat: 
The live chat feature allows customers to contact the team anonymously, like a chatbot 
“Chat Queue” Inbound Chat. “Still in the pipeline” 
4.13- Simple Texting:  
Is a SMS messaging tool we use to communicate with our patients efficiently. It allows 
us to send and receive messages for Next day appointments Confirmation, scheduling, 
rescheduling, cancellations, and answering any inquiries related to our physical therapy 
Practice. 
4.14- Med Answering site: 
MAS “Transportation service provider for Medicaid Patients ONLY, for picking up and 
dropping off patients from – to the physical therapy sessions through prior arrangements 
and bookings through the portal”.

6- Procedures: 
6.1- Help Desk and Virtual Front Desk Processes (Front Line): 
• All agent greets the patients at the location and inquire about How to help the patient? 
• The agent pulls up the patient appointment using DOB, first and last name. 
• All agents are responsible for receiving and fulfilling customer requests as follows: 
6.1.1- Scheduling a new Patient: 
6.1.1.1- The agent starts a new chart for the patient on WebPT and enters the patient’s 
information: (first name, last name, sex, date of birth, insurance name, 
insurance ID, phone number, preferred date). (code: PS/CC-F-01). 
6.1.1.2- The agent notifies the authorization and benefits teams to check the insurance 
coverage of the patient.” Within 10 mins SLA” 
6.1.1.3- The agent is responsible for calling (or sending a text) to the patient to inform 
him of his insurance authorization details (Deductibles, copayment or 
coinsurance, and other details)  
6.1.1.4- The agent checks the referral status of the patient, if the patient does not have a 
referral, the physician’s name in WebPT will be kept as a “direct access”. The 
patient can only have one visit without a referral, a second appointment must 
not be made if the patient has no referral. 
6.1.2- Scheduling an Existing Patient: 
6.1.2.1- The agent verifies patient details, including the first/last name and the DOB. 
6.1.2.2- The agent verifies the authorization status of the patient.  
6.1.2.3- Before scheduling or rescheduling check the visit counter and the authorized 
visits expiry date on the patient chart. 
6.1.2.4- Determine the appropriate appointment category: 
• Follow-up. 
• New Case. 
• Re-examination: last authorized session, first session after authorization 
expiration when the patient did not complete his previous authorized sessions 
or patient absence for 30+ days. 
• Initial examination: patient previously scheduled but did not attend prior 
appointments. 
• One on One: Always scheduled based on the patient request.

6.1.2.5- The agent checks the availability of open slots on the clinic schedule as per the 
green board instructions “How many patients per each 30 minutes” 
6.1.2.6- The agent also verifies the availability of the services required by the patient 
before making an appointment. 
6.1.2.7- The agent should refer to the “services” google sheet (code: PS/CC-A-02) to 
know about services not available at all clinics and who can provide them.  
6.1.2.8- When determining available times, the agent takes into consideration the 
vertical scheduling distribution in clinics by strategically allocating slots 
throughout the whole day for follow-ups and new patient evaluations. This is 
done to minimize load on clinic staff, minimize wait time for patients, prevent 
overbooking, and maintain workflow efficiency. The agent should follow the 
green board notes. 
6.1.2.9- The agent creates the appointment on WebPT and confirms with the patient 
during the call or through SMS that the appointment is confirmed for Day / 
Time. 
6.1.2.10- Exception Handling: 
• If a clinic is overbooked, coordinate with the PT in the clinic for approval to 
see the patient. 
• In case of emergency or patient preferences schedule the patient after 
offering the available slots following the vertical scheduling. 
• Leave a note on the appointment that “Patient Insisted” ”In case of New 
Patients only”

of care. 
Typically, 45-60 minutes per session. 
Patients can have multiple follow-ups in a week, depending 
on their treatment plan. “Max 3 sessions / week”

6.1.3- Cancelling: 
6.1.3.1- The agent opens the schedule and select the patient’s appointment on WebPT. 
6.1.3.2- The agent determines the cancellation type by selecting “Cancel” for a future 
appointment or “No Show” if the cancellation is on the same day. 
6.1.3.3- The agent documents the reason for cancellation from the list of reasons, if the 
reason is not found, the agent selects “Other” from the list with document any 
addition details in notes in cancellation reasons form (code: PS/CC-F-02). The 
reason must be documented thoroughly. 
6.1.3.4- The agent shall notify the patient that their appointment has been cancelled in 
the call.

6.1.3.5- In case the patient does not have upcoming appointment the agent should 
schedule and upcoming appointment for the patient: “When would you like to 
reschedule another appointment?” “Unless the patient mentioned that he will 
call back to schedule, will be travelling, having some concerns etc.” 
6.1.3.6- The agent suggests available dates and times for the next appointment. 
6.1.4- Transportation Handling: 
6.1.4.1- The agent receives the transportation request via front desk as a task assigned 
on Click Up (code: PS/CC-F-03), phone call from the patients requesting to 
schedule transportation.  
6.1.4.2- The agent makes sure the patient is Medicaid eligible. 
6.1.4.3- If the request was over the phone from a patient requesting transportation, then 
the agent collects all required details (first & last name – date of birth – 
Medicaid ID – pick-up and return address – pick-up and return time – taxi or 
wheelchair request – preferred car agency – referring physician name). 
6.1.4.4- If the request was via Click Up ticket, then the front desk must mention the 
below info (EMR ID – clinic name -first & last name – date of birth – Medicaid 
ID – pick-up and return address – pick-up and return time – taxi or wheelchair 
request – preferred car agency – referring physician name). 
6.1.4.5- The agent makes sure all fields are complete and accurate before submission. 
6.1.4.6- On dashboard on med answering site (code: PS/CC-F-04) the agent searches 
using the date of birth and Medicaid ID or last name to verify patient 
information  
6.1.4.7- The agent enters all trip details (pick-up/drop-off locations, provider, and clinic 
info. 
6.1.4.8- The agent chooses the correct transportation provider according to the preferred 
car agency or request and in case of no preferences the agent leaves it as no for 
the MAS company to decide which provider will be providing the service.  
6.1.4.9- The agent submits the booking on med answering site.

6.1.4.10- The agent confirms and makes sure the trip is successfully booked. 
6.1.4.11- the agent copies the confirmation details and adds them to Click-Up 
transportation (code: PS/CC-F-03)  and the shared google spaces between the 
customer care and the front desk. 
NOTE: Practice solution only deals with “MAS” company for transportation. 
6.1.5- General Inquiries: 
6.1.5.1- The agent refers to the clinic’s info sheet (code: PS/CC-F-05) or the website for 
information on addresses, working days/hours. 
6.1.5.2- For service-related inquiries, the agent provides general information on 
common services. 
6.1.5.3- If unsure whether a specific service is offered, the agent confirms with the 
clinic or with the operations team. 
6.1.5.4- If further clarification is needed, the agent escalates the inquiry to clinical 
management for confirmation. 
6.1.6- Complaint Calls: 
6.1.6.1- This call type in specific is handled by the patient pulse team, so the calls are to 
be directed to them. 
6.1.6.2- The agent should actively listen to the patient and demonstrate care and 
empathy. 
6.1.6.3- The agent expresses understanding and apologizes for any inconvenience 
caused. 
6.1.6.4- The agent tries to resolve the issue as much as possible within their capacity. 
6.1.6.5- At the end of the call, the agent informs the patient that their complaint will be 
escalated to top management for deeper and high-level investigation and 
promise with a call back after doing the investigations. 
6.1.6.6- The agent documents the complaint in patient pulse unit sheet (code: PS/CC-F-
06) and create a ticket with it for either proper follow up or to be assigned to 
the concerned department accordingly for issue resolution.  
6.1.6.7- Call back with the investigations results to be done to the patient updating him 
with the results and the corrective actions taken.

6.1.7- Zocdoc & PT Of the City Website Module Follow Up: 
6.1.7.1- The front-line agent shall verify the patient information on Zocdoc 
appointments. 
6.1.7.2- In case of the New Patients Bookings:  
• The agent creates the patient chart on System WebPT. 
• The agent Logs all the patient chart information on the Referrals CRM system. 
• The agent sends the information needed to the Benefits team. 
• The agent shall check the schedule of clinic to put the patient at the mentioned 
date & time. 
6.1.7.3- Once the benefits team confirmed the patient status the agent should call or 
text the patient with the benefits provided, if the patient approved then the agent 
to confirm with the patient the date and time of the appointment. 
6.1.7.4- The agent confirms the appointment on Zocdoc. 
6.1.8- Simple Texting Follow Up: 
6.1.8.1- Back Office agent has unique user accounts to work on the simple texting app. 
6.1.8.2- The contact details of all existing customers are saved in the system, allowing 
their names to appear automatically when they send a message using the phone 
number. 
6.1.8.3- The agent should review the customer’s name and previous conversation 
history before responding to ensure a personalized experience. 
6.1.8.4- This is the responsibility of the entire team to ensure the SMS queue reaches 
zero backlog by the end of the day, with the supervisor responsible for 
overseeing and following up on the entire team to ensure this is achieved. 
6.2- Back-office Processes: 
6.2.1- No Upcoming Calls: 
6.2.1.1-The agent contacts patients who had an appointment yesterday but either 
canceled, no-showed, or attended without scheduling another appointment.

6.2.1.2- The agent greets the patient warmly, asks how they are doing, and shows care 
and concern, especially for those who canceled or missed their appointment. 
6.2.1.3- The agent politely asks the patient when would like to schedule a new 
appointment to continue their treatment for better health. 
6.2.1.4- If the patient agrees, the agent asks for their preferred date and offers the 
available time slot based on the green board and the vertical scheduling. 
6.2.1.5- If the patient declines, the agent asks if they have any comments or concerns 
about the service and promise the patient with a call back form the complaints 
department to take care of his issue. 
6.2.1.6- Then the case to be assigned directly to the Patient Pulse Unit to contact the 
patient, address his issues, escalate it whenever required to retain the patient. 
6.2.2- Appointment Confirmation SMS and Filtration: 
6.2.2.1- Appointment confirmation is done via SMS using simple texting, back-office 
agents filter responses to SMS and records results on the dashboard Form. 
Actions are taken on WebPT depending on the SMS response (eg: rescheduling 
or cancelling and appointment). 
6.2.3- Appointment Confirmation Calls: 
6.2.3.1- The agent calls patients to confirm the next day appointments.  
6.2.3.2- The agent provides the appointment date, time, and clinic name during the call. 
6.2.3.3- If the patient wishes to reschedule, the agent follows the standard rescheduling 
procedure. 
NOTE: This step is performed after the campaign text confirmation. Calls are made only 
to patients who did not receive, reply or opted out of text message notifications. 
6.2.4- Non-AV: 
6.2.4.1- Back office makes outgoing calls based on the non-AV reports generated by the 
Authorization department every day. They inform patients of the insurance hold 
and reschedule visits accordingly.

6.3- Processes of Data entry team: 
6.3.1- New patients:   
6.3.1.1- Data entry officer details of new patients from WebPT and inputs them onto the 
new patients google sheet (code: PS/CC-F-07). 
6.3.1.2- Data entry team obtains the details of each “checked out” visit and enter it onto 
the eligibility sheets (code: PS/B&A-F-01). These sheets are then shared with 
the authorization department. 
6.4- Virtual Front Desk:  
6.4.1- The agent must make sure that the patient fills and signs all the patient intake forms 
(code: PS/CC-F-08), on arrival to the first visit, which includes patient 
information, insurance information, WC or no fault information, cancellation and 
no-show policy, patient medical history, authorization to release and obtain 
information, general consent to treatment, notice of privacy practices, insurance 
eligibility disclaimer, general insurance consent, financial responsibility, virtual 
support services consent, termination of the provider-patient relationship, 
assignment and release of benefits, communication attestation, and photo / video 
release form. The patient should also sign the cupping consent form if the patient 
will undergo cupping therapy, and the pelvic examination and treatment consent 
form if the patient will undergo pelvic examination or treatment. 
6.4.2- The agent inputs all information, signed documents and scheduled visits into 
WebPT, including any special considerations such as the patient’s preferred 
language and communication needs (e.g., interpreter). The agent registers the entry 
time of the patient to the clinic on the patient tracker. 
6.5- Account Manager Process: 
6.5.1- Input data to daily schedule tracker sheet (code: PS/CC-F-09) from WebPT 
schedule everyday 
6.5.2- Virtual front desk or PCCs enter other data based on actual visit data every two 
hours. 
6.5.3- Account Managers review data that PCC entered using WebPT charts to make sure 
people are doing their jobs right.

I understand and acknowledge the importance of attending scheduled appointments and giving timely
notice for cancellations or rescheduling when necessary. By signing below, I attest to the following:
1.Late Cancellation Policy: I agree to provide at least 24 hours' notice if I need to cancel or reschedule my
appointment. I understand that this allows the healthcare provider to offer the appointment slot to another
patient in need of care.
2.No-Show Policy: I understand that failing to attend a scheduled appointment without prior notification
constitutes a "no-show." I acknowledge the disruptive impact of no-shows on the healthcare provider's
schedule and the ability to provide timely care to other patients. 3. Consequences:
•First Late Cancellation/No-Show: I understand that for the first instance of a no show, a reminder will be
issued, and no charges will apply.
•Subsequent Late Cancellation/No-Shows: I understand that for subsequent instances of no-shows, fees may
be charged to my account as below, and my future scheduling may be subject to review.
No-Show: $10.
Late Cancellation: $10.
4.Communication: I agree to promptly inform the healthcare provider of any changes to my contact
information to ensure I receive appointment reminders and important updates. To cancel or reschedule my
appointment, I agree to contact PT of the City at (718) 648-0888.
5. Exceptions:
•Emergencies: I understand that emergencies happen, and if I am unable to make my appointment due to
unforeseen circumstances, I will notify the healthcare provider as soon as possible.
•Special Circumstances: I acknowledge that if I have concerns or special circumstances regarding my
appointment, I will contact the healthcare provider to discuss alternative arrangements.
By signing below, I acknowledge that I have read and understood the cancellation and no-show policy
outlined above. I agree to adhere to these policies and understand the consequences of failing to do so.

Cupping therapy is a form of alternative medicine in which a therapist puts special cups on your skin for a few
minutes to create suction. It is used for many purposes, including to help with pain , inﬂammation , blood ﬂow ,
relaxation and well-being , and as a type of deep-tissue massage. There is almost no risk of use and is similar
to any other form of soft tissue work (i.e. massage or Garston) . However, there may be risk of discoloration
where the cups were placed . This is a voluntarily treatment option they can be accessed by your therapist with
your consent. In order to be prepared in the event of this treatment is desired by your therapist , you are
receiving this proactively for permission to utilize Cupping Therapy by the PT of The City team member. The
patient &parent/guardian must sign this consent form if you agree to its terms and conditions :

reaction occurs from the release and clearing of stagnation and toxins from the body.
I further understand that these discolorations will dissipate anywhere from a few hours to as long as tw o
weeks.

1- Purpose: 
To define a standardized procedure for managing data requests, generating reports, maintaining 
data quality, and providing analytical support to all departments. This procedure ensures 
accuracy, consistency, security, and timely delivery of Data & BI services. 
2- Scope: 
This procedure applies to all activities related to data extraction requests, dashboard and report 
creation and updates, data validation and quality assurance, database maintenance and updates, 
business intelligence platform administration (including Power BI, Tableau, Looker, and 
internal dashboards), data pipelines and automated workflows, as well as ad-hoc analytics and 
forecasting requests. 
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- BI: Business Intelligence. 
4.2- ETL / ELT: Extract, Transform, Load processes. 
4.3- Dataset: Collection of structured data used for reporting. 
4.4- Dashboard: Visual summary of KPIs and metrics. 
4.5- Data Quality: Accuracy, consistency, and completeness of data.

1- Purpose: 
To provide a clear and standardized procedure for managing all employee benefits, ensuring 
transparency, compliance, and employee satisfaction. 
2- Scope: 
This procedure applies to all activities related to the administration, management, and 
maintenance of employee benefits within the organization. The scope covers the full life cycle 
of benefits programs, including but not limited to medical insurance, social insurance, leaves, 
allowances, transportation support, and any other benefits offered as part of the total rewards 
strategy. 
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
3.2- labor law 2025 and insurance law no. 148 2019 
4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- Employee Benefits: 
Non-wage compensations provided to employees in addition to their normal salaries or 
wages (e.g., insurance, allowances, leaves). 
4.2- Medical Insurance:

4.4- Benefit Provider: 
The external party or vendor responsible for offering and managing the benefit service 
(e.g., insurance company, government office). 
4.5- Benefit Cycle: 
The timeline covering benefit initiation, updates, renewal, and closure within a specific 
period (e.g., annually).

is the official document used to register a new employee with the Social Insurance

6.1- Eligibility Overview Benefits: 
6.1.1- Medical insurance: Eligible after successful completion of the probation period (as

employment.  
6.2- Social Insurance Enrollment: 
6.2.1- Submit Form 1. 
6.2.2- Maintain updated reports of insured employees. 
6.2.3- Ensure full compliance with Egyptian labor law. 
6.3- Medical Insurance Enrollment (Post-Probation): 
6.3.1- Monitor the employee’s probation period closely. 
6.3.2- Upon the employee signs the contract (code: PS/HR-F-01):  
6.3.2.1- Verify the confirmation letter signed by the general manager and HR Manager. 
6.3.2.2- Register the employee in the medical insurance list within 10 working days after 
probation. 
6.3.4- Submit the employee’s details and any dependent information to the insurance 
provider.  
6.4- Allowances and Other Benefits: 
6.4.1- Review employee’s eligibility based on position and department. 
6.5- Employee Communication and Support: 
6.5.1- Inform new employees during onboarding that medical insurance is activated after 
probation period. 
6.5.2- Respond to benefit inquiries within 2 working days. 
6.5.3- Collect feedback after activation to evaluate provider performance by email. 
6.6- Benefits Termination: 
6.6.1- Upon employee resignation or termination: 
6.6.1.1- Submit form 6 for social insurance cancellation.

Maintenance of Documented Information Procedure 
Ref # 
PS/DOC-P-01 
Issue # 
01 
Issue Date 
01.01.2026 
Rev # 
0 
Quality Assurance Department 
Rev Date 
00.00.0000 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Page 2 of 9 
CONTENTS: 
1- Purpose 
2- Scope 
3- References 
4. Terms / Terminology 
5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 
6- Procedures 
7- Revision / Amendment Log 
8- Document Distribution List 
9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 
10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 
11- Appendices and Forms 
 
1- Purpose: 
This document clarifies the processes followed by the company for creating, modifying and 
identifying documents, including externally originated documented information, related to 
the quality management system. This document also describes the processes through which 
these documents are issued and revised within the company. 
2- Scope: 
2.1- The preparation, issuance, review, modification, classification and identification of 
documented information within the quality management system across all levels 
(procedures, work instructions/SOPs, forms, etc.). 
2.2- The maintenance and updating of externally originated documented information. 
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
4- Terms / Terminology: 
4.1- Documented Information: 
Information that requires control within the company, including the type of medium in 
which it is contained. 
4.2- Procedure: 
A document that explains how and in what sequence a specific overall process is 
performed. It is considered a second-level document in the hierarchy of the quality 
management system documents.
page_299:

01

Quality Assurance Department 
Rev Date 
00.00.0000 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Page 3 of 9 
4.3- Work Instructions / SOPs: 
A document that explains the sequential execution of a detailed sub-process within an 
overall process and is considered a third-level document in the hierarchy of the quality 
management system. 
4.4- Form:  
A form is a structured document or digital interface used to collect, organize, or submit 
information in a standardized way. 
4.5- Record: 
A document containing the results of completed activities, serving as tangible evidence 
that these activities were performed. Typically, records are completed forms that require 
retention. 
4.6- Appendix: 
An appendix is a document that adds information that supports another document by 
adding information or data not essential for understanding the process, appendices often 
include process flowcharts or tables of data. 
4.7- Document Identification and Classification: 
Defining and distinguishing the document by a unique name and code for each document 
within the scope of the management system. 
4.8- Document: 
Refers to all copies of maintained documented information, including all revisions. 
5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation: 
Title 
Responsibility 
CEO 
Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this 
procedure achieve their intended outcomes. 
Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are 
integrated within business processes. 
Head of Operational 
Excellence 
Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this 
procedure are implemented and described, and for ensuring 
that any changes made to controlled documents are reviewed 
and approved. 
Quality Assurance 
Manager 
Responsible for reviewing all documents within the scope of 
this procedure to ensure its conformance with standards 
requirements and reviews its business impact. 
All Managers 
and Supervisors 
Responsible for the preparation and correct use of all 
documents related to their departments, and yearly revision in 
accordance with the guidelines outlined in this procedure.

6.1- Practice Solution for Programing management system document hierarchy is 
as follows:  
6.1.1- Level One: management system elements: 
1. Practice Solution Quality Policy: PS/QA-POL shall be prepared and distributed in 
all Practice Solution facilities and departments and understood by all interested 
parties. 
2. Practice Solution Organization Chart: PS/HR-ORG including management 
levels, personnel and facilities under the Practice Solution umbrella.  
6.1.2- Level Two: Procedures. 
6.1.3- Level Three: Work instructions / SOPs.  
6.1.4- Level Four: Forms and appendices. 
6.2- Identification and Classification of Management System Documents within 
the Company:

6.3- Document Creation Process: 
6.3.1- The department manager or his designee begins document preparation in 
coordination with the document control officer until the final draft is ready. The 
department manager submits the document along with a document issue request, 
(code: PS/DOC-F-01) The document control officer reviews formatting to make 
sure all documents follow the same formatting patterns. 
• When preparing procedures: 
1- Purpose. 
2- Scope. 
3- References. 
4. Terms / Terminology. 
5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation. 
6- Procedures. 
7- Revision / Amendment Log (created by the document control officer). 
8- Document Distribution List (created by the document control officer). 
9- Procedure Execution Time / SLA. 
10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement. 
11- Appendices and Forms. 
12- A swim lane process flowchart as an appendix to the procedure. 
Typeface: Times New Roman, font for subprocess titles: 14pts bold, font for body: 13pts

• When preparing Work Instructions / SOPs: 
1- Purpose / Objective. 
2- References. 
3- Responsibility for Implementation. 
4- Detailed Instructions. 
5- Revision / Amendment Log (created by the document control officer). 
6- Document Distribution List (created by the document control officer). 
7- Execution time / SLA. 
8- KPIs. 
9- Forms and Appendices. 
Typeface: Times New Roman, font for subprocess titles: 13pts bold, font for body: 12pts 
• When preparing Forms: 
Forms are reviewed by the document control officer to ensure they follow the same 
formatting patterns (where appropriate). 
6.3.2- The final draft is submitted to the document control officer, who ensures its 
identification, coding, formatting, and structuring.  
6.3.3- The document control officer submits the document for review by the quality 
assurance manager, and top management. 
6.3.4- The document control officer changes the issue status by changing the revision 
number and date when any part of the document is changed, edited, or amended. 
6.3.5- After the final draft is identified and reviewed, the document control officer gets the 
documents signed by the manager of the process owner department, the quality 
assurance manager, and the top management. 
6.4- Document Distribution, Revision, Cancellation, and Control: 
6.4.1- The document control officer is responsible for preserving the original stamped 
copy of the documented information. The original copy is stamped “Issued 
Document”. Other copies are distributed to associated department managers based 
on the document distribution list. Physically distributed copies are stamped 
accordingly, and evidence of distribution is recorded on the document receipt form 
(code: PS/DOC-F-08). When distributing a revised copy, any old copies must be 
withdrawn by the document control officer so that revised and old versions do not 
coexist. 
6.4.2- Heads of departments must perform yearly reviews to each document within the 
scope of their department to verify continued fitness for purpose and must review 
documents when changes occur. Whenever documents need updating, the 
department manager sends a request (code: PS/DOC-F-01) to the document control 
officer and includes the updates/changes to the document. The department manager 
and document control officer then follow the creation process outlined in 6.3 of this 
document. Revised documents (yearly revisions) are given a new revision number 
and are re-approved, even if no changes occur due to the department manager 
revision.

6.4.3- Documents are cancelled when a new revision or issue is approved, or when a 
cancellation request is approved by the quality assurance manager. The document 
control officer must retain a copy of cancelled or amended documents for at least 
three years. Cancelled documents are marked with the “Cancelled” impression or 
watermark and recorded on the cancelled documents list, code: PS/DOC-F-07.

6.5- Delivery of Management System Documents to External Parties: 
- If any external party requests documents – based on a written agreement, contract,
or to meet regulatory or legal requirements – the quality assurance manager may
provide the requested copies of documents to the external party after obtaining
prior approval from the top management. The copy must be stamped “uncontrolled
copy”.
6.6- Retention of records: 
- Relevant department managers are responsible for retaining all forms and records
for at least 3 years, or as mentioned in their procedures.

1- Purpose: 
This procedure aims to standardize how the Operational Excellence (OpEx) department 
receives, evaluates, executes, and tracks support requests to maintain quality, accountability, 
and continuous improvement across all functions. 
2- Scope: 
2.1- This procedure applies to all support activities provided by the OpEx department to 
internal departments, business units, and senior management. 
2.2- It covers all requests related to process optimization, performance analytics, project 
facilitation, documentation, system improvement, and general operational support. 
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- Support Request:  
A formal request submitted to the OpEx department seeking assistance, guidance, or 
intervention related to operational performance or process improvement. 
4.2- OpEx: 
Operational Excellence Department – responsible for driving efficiency, quality, and 
process standardization across the organization.

4.3- SLA: 
Service Level Agreement – defines the target response and completion times for various 
request types. 
4.4- KPI:  
Key Performance Indicator is used to measure the effectiveness and timeliness of the 
support process. 
4.5- Requester:  
The department or individual submitting a support request to OpEx. 
4.5- Support Lead:  
The designated OpEx team member responsible for coordinating the request handling and 
execution. (ex. Project manager, Quality manager & Compliance manager) 
5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation: 
Title 
Responsibility 
CEO 
Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this 
procedure achieve their intended outcomes. 
Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are 
integrated within business processes. 
Head of Operational 
Excellence 
Owns this procedure, ensures its implementation, assign 
requests, approves revisions, and monitors compliance and 
quality reports and outcomes. 
OpEx Support lead

requesters, document activities, and adhere to defined SLAs. 
Requesting Departments 
/ Individuals 
Submit clear and complete support requests, provide 
necessary data or access, and collaborate during execution. 
Quality Assurance 
Audits the quality of the procedures and how each 
department work within these procedures and verifies 
documentation and record preservation.

6- Procedures: 
6.1- Request Submission: 
6.1.1- All support requests must be submitted through the official “Support Request Form” 
(code: PS/OpEx-F-01) or via the company’s internal communication platform or 
email. 
6.1.2- Requests must include a clear description, objective, expected outcome, and any 
relevant data or attachments. 
6.2- Request Review and Prioritization: 
6.2.1- The OpEx team reviews requests within 2:5 business days of submission. 
6.2.2- Requests are assessed based on urgency, impact, alignment with strategic priorities, 
and available capacity. 
6.2.3- The head of opex approves or assigns priority levels and the support lead who will 
works on this task. 
6.3- Assignment and Execution: 
6.3.1- A support lead is assigned to coordinate the task.  
6.3.2- The support lead prepares an action plan form (code: PS/OpEx-F-02) or timeline 
based on the request type. 
6.3.3- Progress updates are communicated to the requester periodically. 
6.4- Validation and Closure: 
6.4.1- Upon completion, the requester reviews and confirms satisfaction. 
6.4.2- Documentation, templates, and outcomes are archived in the OpEx knowledge base. 
6.4.3- Feedback is collected and recorded for continuous improvement in Monthly Strategic 
form (code: PS/PM-F-01) 
6.5- Escalation and Exception Handling: 
6.5.1- Any delay, issue, or deviation from SLA must be reported immediately to the head of 
OpEx via email. 
6.5.2- Corrective or preventive actions are logged and tracked as part of quality 
improvement efforts and will be handled by the quality assurance.

1- Purpose: 
The purpose of this document is to define the standard operating procedures and processes 
for the AI Department at the company for the development, implementation, and 
maintenance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions. This ensures a consistent, high-quality, 
and ethical approach to leveraging AI to enhance exclusive services, optimize operational 
efficiency, and deliver superior value to our client.  
2- Scope: 
This procedure applies to all projects within the AI Department. The scope covers all phases, 
from initial concept generation and feasibility study through development, testing, 
deployment, and post-implementation maintenance for AI solutions. 
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- AI Solution: 
A system or application that uses AI/Machine Learning techniques (e.g., NLP, predictive 
modeling) to solve a business problem.  
4.2- POC: 
Proof of Concept. A small-scale, internal project to test the viability of a proposed AI 
approach.  
4.3- MVP: 
Minimum Viable Product. A version of the solution with just enough features to be usable 
by early customers/internal teams for feedback.

Page 3 of 6 
4.4- HIPAA: 
Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act. US legislation providing data privacy 
and security provisions for medical information. 
4.5- SME: 
Subject Matter Expert. A person with deep knowledge in a specific company service area 
(e.g., Medical Billing). 
4.6- Pipeline: 
The end-to-end process from data ingestion and cleaning to model training, deployment, 
and monitoring. 
4.7- Bias: 
Systematic error in a model that causes it to produce inaccurate or unfair results across 
different groups. 
4.8- Client:  
Any individual, group, or organization that receives a product, service, or specific output 
that is intended for or required by the client. A client can be internal to the company or 
external to it. 
5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation: 
Title 
Responsibility

AI Department 
Personnel

maintaining records, and ensuring all work meets quality, ethical, 
and security standards. 
IT Department 
Provides and maintains the necessary infrastructure and 
deployment environment for the AI solutions. 
Department Heads 
(Service Delivery) 
Responsible for identifying business problems, providing SMEs, 
validating solution performance, and ensuring adoption. 
Data Governance 
Officer 
Ensures data used for training and testing is compliant with 
HIPAA.

Page 4 of 6 
6- Procedures: 
6.1- Problem Identification & Feasibility 
6.1.1- Idea Generation & Intake (Form PS/AI-F-01) 
Business needs are captured via a formal request or identified during strategy 
meetings. The request details the problem, business impact, and potential AI value. 
6.1.2- Initial Assessment 
AI Lead and relevant SME evaluate technical feasibility, data availability/quality, 
compliance risks, and alignment with strategic goals. 
 
6.1.3- Decision Gate 1 (Go/No-Go) 
AI Manager and relevant department head approve proceeding to POC. 
6.2- Proof of Concept (POC) & Design 
6.2.1- Data Acquisition & Preparation 
Data Scientists work with the Data Governance Officer to securely acquire and 
clean a representative, HIPAA-compliant dataset. 
6.2.2- Model Exploration & POC 
AI Engineers/Data Scientists rapidly develop and test initial model architectures. 
Focus is on demonstrating the core hypothesis that can be solved with AI. 
6.2.3- Architecture Design 
Based on successful POC, detailed architecture and system design documents are 
created, including integration points and required infrastructure. 
6.2.4- Decision Gate 2 (POC Approval) 
AI Manager and IT approve the POC, confirming infrastructure readiness and 
resource allocation.  
6.3- Development & Iteration 
6.3.1- Full Data Pipeline Development 
The complete, production-ready data ingestion, transformation, and storage pipeline 
is built. 
6.3.2- Model Training & Optimization 
Models are rigorously trained, optimized, and tested against defined performance 
metrics, and simultaneously evaluated for bias, with initial validation performed to 
ensure consistency and alignment with the defined inputs and input specifications. 
6.3.3- Internal Testing & Verification 
The solution is tested for functionality, performance, and robustness by the AI and 
IT teams. This includes extensive back-testing against historical data and 
verification that the solution behaves correctly and consistently with the defined 
inputs and input specifications.

Page 5 of 6 
6.4- Pilot & Deployment 
6.4.1- Pilot Preparation 
The solution is integrated into a controlled, non-critical production environment. 
6.4.2- Pilot Execution (MVP Launch) 
The solution is deployed as an MVP to a small, isolated group of users or for a 
specific service line for the client (e.g., a subset of Medical Auditing cases). 
6.4.3- User Feedback & Performance Monitoring 
Continuous monitoring and collection of user feedback are performed. Model 
performance is tracked against real-time operational metrics. It is recorded in AI 
Piloting form code PS/AI-F-02 
6.4.4 Change Requests Documentation: 
Any change requested by the client during the pilot phase must be formally 
documented using the same request form code (PS/AI-F-01), completed as a new 
change-request sheet and attached to the original request form code (PS/AI-F-01) 
6.4.5- Decision Gate 3 (Production Readiness) 
Successful pilot completion, approval by the AI Manager and the relevant client. 
6.5- Maintenance & Improvement 
6.5.1- Ongoing Monitoring 
A dedicated team monitors model performance for Drift (degradation in prediction 
accuracy over time) and system health. It is recorded in AI Outputs Monitoring 
Form code (PS/AI-F-04). 
6.5.2- Retraining & Updates 
Model retraining may be initiated as a decision resulting from the monitoring 
activities. When this is the case, it is documented on the AI Monitoring Plan Form 
code (PS/AI-F-03). 
 6.5.3- Feature Enhancement 
New feature requests and improvement iterations feed back into Process 1, 
ensuring the solution evolves with clients and our partners' needs.

1- Purpose: 
The purpose of this procedure is to establish a structured virtual field operations process that 
provides continuous remote supervision of clinic operations from opening to closing. This 
procedure ensures real-time operational, quality, and compliance control, enables proactive 
identification and resolution of issues, supports data-driven performance monitoring and 
reporting, and defines clear escalation and follow-up mechanisms across all involved 
departments. 
2- Scope: 
This procedure applies to all RED Agents responsible for remote clinic oversight and covers 
monitoring and support of clinic operational readiness and opening compliance; patient flow 
processes including check-in, check-out, next appointment scheduling, authorizations, and 
patient surveys; Virtual Front Desk and PCC non-clinical performance impacting clinic 
operations; staff presence, schedule adherence, and coverage; safety, regulatory, and visual 
HIPAA compliance; coordination of vendors, maintenance, facilities, and logistics; and 
tracking clinic performance KPIs with identification and escalation of performance 
deviations. 
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
3.2- Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA).

6.1- Incident Escalation and Communication Guidelines : 
6.1.1- RED Agents are authorized to contact any department to request immediate 
corrective action when an incident is identified. 
6.1.2- All communications, incidents, and escalations will be logged, timestamped, and 
evidence-based. 
6.1.3- RED Agents will follow up until resolution is verified. 
6.1.4- RED Agents do not provide clinical direction and do not take disciplinary actions. 
6.1.5- Agents use the following communication channels to communicate with process 
owners: 
- VOICE – Incident reporting and escalations 
- Google Chat Spaces – Immediate operational communication 
- Intermedia – Clinic call documentation

1- Purpose: 
To define a systematic approach for identifying, developing, delivering, and evaluating 
training that effectively addresses business, performance, and compliance needs within the 
company, ensuring staff competency and contributing to the achievement of departmental 
and company objectives. 
2- Scope: 
2.1- This procedure applies to all training programs developed, delivered, or managed by the 
Learning & Development (L&D) department for all employees of the company. 
2.2- It covers the entire training lifecycle from initial need identification through to post-
training evaluation and record keeping. 
3- References: 
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001:2015. 
4- Terms / Terminology:  
4.1- Training Needs Analysis (TNA): 
A systematic process of identifying the gaps between current and desired knowledge, 
skills, and abilities, and determining the training required to close these gaps.

4.2- Learning Management System (LMS): 
A software application for the administration, documentation, tracking, reporting, and 
delivery of educational courses or training programs. 
4.3- Time-to-Proficiency: 
The period from an employee's start date or role change to the point where they can 
perform all job functions independently and to the required standard. 
5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 
Title 
Responsibility 
CEO 
Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this procedure 
achieve their intended outcomes. 
Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are 
integrated within business processes.

expertise. 
L&D

evaluation, and record keeping as outlined in this procedure. 
Employees 
Responsible for active participation in assigned training and providing 
constructive feedback. 
 
6- Procedures: 
6.1- Training Need Identification: 
6.1.1- A training need is identified reactively via a formal training need request (code: 
PS/L&D-F-01) submitted by a department head or the QA department.
